Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Role Title: Machine Learning Engineer

Role Type: Contractor

Location: Remote




micro1 is engaging Machine Learning Engineers to contribute expertise to a dynamic customer project. In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.




Scope of Work

Design, develop, and refine machine learning models using Python and relevant libraries to address project objectives.
Analyze large datasets and leverage MongoDB to manage and retrieve data efficiently for model training and validation.
Collaborate with cross-functional contributors to identify areas for model improvement and implement robust solutions.
Conduct thorough model evaluation, tuning hyperparameters, and benchmarking results to ensure optimal performance.
Document methodologies, experiments, and outcomes to ensure transparent and repeatable workflows.
Integrate data pipelines and preprocessing workflows to streamline training and inference processes.
Deliver actionable insights and recommendations based on data-driven findings and machine learning outcomes.




Preferred Qualifications

Demonstrated expertise with Python, including deep familiarity with machine learning frameworks such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
Hands-on experience with MongoDB for data manipulation, storage, and retrieval within machine learning projects.
Strong problem-solving skills and a track record of delivering innovative ML solutions in real-world settings.
Understanding of model evaluation metrics, feature engineering, and effective data preprocessing techniques.
Background in deploying or operationalizing machine learning models in cloud or enterprise environments.
Clear written documentation and communication skills for sharing technical findings and best practices.
Ability to adapt quickly to evolving project requirements and contribute collaboratively in a remote setting.

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Consultant - Data Scientist
Description:
Over Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is een Brussels consultancybureau gespecialiseerd in Data en Artificial Intelligence. Wij begeleiden onze partners bij complexe, vaak unieke uitdagingen via consultance, audits en het ontwikkelen van op maat gemaakte oplossingen. Onze aanpak is bewust holistisch: we laten Data Engineering, Advanced Analytics en AI samenkomen, want wij geloven erin dat daar de échte impact ontstaat.




Bij Exsolvæ noemen we onze consultants Solvers; gepassioneerde professionals die met de nieuwste technologieën werken, blijven leren, kennis delen, en gedreven zijn om duurzame oplossingen met écht resultaat te leveren.




Over de rol

Ergens in een berg ruwe data zit een antwoord verstopt dat een organisatie vooruit kan stuwen, en jij bent degene die het eruit haalt. Als Data Scientist ga je voorbij het beschrijven van wat er gebeurde, en richt je je op het voorspellen van wat komen gaat. Je bouwt modellen die patronen zien waar mensen ze missen, je vertaalt vage businessvragen naar scherpe hypotheses, en je zet statistiek en machine learning in om beslissingen te onderbouwen die er écht toe doen. Geen modellen voor de showcase, maar oplossingen die in productie het verschil maken.




Wat ga je doen?

Machine learning-modellen ontwerpen, trainen en finetunen die concrete businessvragen beantwoorden
Ruwe, rommelige data omtoveren tot features waar een model écht iets mee kan
Voorspellende en prescriptieve analyses bouwen die verder kijken dan wat al gebeurd is
Experimenteren met statistische methodes, A/B-tests en hypothesetoetsing om inzichten hard te maken
Modellen van prototype naar productie brengen, met oog voor performance, schaalbaarheid en MLOps
Python en de bijbehorende ecosystemen (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow) inzetten om ideeën tot leven te brengen
Databricks en Spark gebruiken om modellen op grote datasets te trainen en te laten draaien
Werken met data in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure en/of AWS om ML-workflows op te zetten en te schalen
Resultaten vertalen naar heldere verhalen en visualisaties waar ook niet-techneuten mee verder kunnen
Bijdragen aan data-standaarden, governance en best practices binnen de organisatie




Wat breng je mee?

4+ jaar ervaring als Data Scientist of in een gelijkwaardige rol
Sterke expertise in machine learning, van model design tot deployment in productie
Gevorderde kennis van Python en SQL, met een neus voor efficiënte, schaalbare code
Solide statistische basis; je weet wanneer een resultaat betekenisvol is en wanneer het ruis is
Hands-on ervaring met Databricks en Spark
Ervaring in Azure- en/of AWS-omgevingen
Bewezen ervaring met het naar productie brengen van modellen (MLOps, model monitoring)
Kennis van moderne ML-frameworks (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow) en idealiter ervaring met GenAI/LLM's
Een analytische, nieuwsgierige mindset; je blijft graven tot het antwoord klopt
Sterke communicatieve skills; je vertaalt complexe modellen naar verhalen die blijven hangen
Vloeiend Engels en Nederlands en idealiter spreek je ook een woordje Frans (B1/B2)




Waarom Exsolvæ?

Projecten met écht impact, geen werk dat in een la verdwijnt
Een innovatieve omgeving waarin leren en kennisdeling in het DNA zitten
Volop ruimte om te groeien en jezelf opnieuw uit te vinden
Hybride werken; flexibel schakelen tussen kantoor en thuis
Een competitief salaris met een mooi pakket aan voordelen
Klinkt dit als jouw volgende uitdaging? Laat het me weten en ik vertel je er met plezier meer over!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We are seeking a driven and creative Data Scientist with a minimum of 3 years of experience in a data science or machine learning role, who will be instrumental in building predictive models, testing hypotheses, and providing statistically sound insights that enhance our lead generation capabilities. The Data Scientist will be part of our analytics team, working closely with IT, marketing, media buying and CRO teams to transform raw data into actionable insights, whether that means building real-time scoring models, investigating campaign performance, or helping the business distinguish signal from noise when making decisions.

The ideal candidate is someone who thrives on solving ambiguous problems, brings strong statistical intuition alongside machine learning skills, and can serve as a trusted analytical partner to stakeholders across the business. They're as comfortable building production ML pipelines as they are answering a media buyer's question about whether a source is actually underperforming or just experiencing variance. Experience in digital marketing, lead generation, or ad-tech is highly desirable.

Primary Responsibilities


Leverage agentic AI-assisted development tools throughout the workflow, from exploratory analysis and feature engineering to model development and pipeline building, to accelerate iteration speed while maintaining rigor and code quality
Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models to segment and score landing page visitors based on intent and lead quality, using behavioral signals such as scroll depth, click patterns, session duration, and form engagement.
Engineer predictive features from raw user behavior data to improve model accuracy and business relevance.
Build and maintain real-time scoring pipelines that enable dynamic postback signals to ad networks, improving campaign optimization speed and efficiency.
Collaborate with marketing and analytics teams to define quality/intent tiers and translate business logic into quantifiable model outputs.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and experimentation to uncover patterns in user behavior that correlate with downstream lead quality and conversion outcomes.
Continuously monitor, evaluate, and iterate on model performance, ensuring alignment with evolving business goals and traffic patterns.
Contribute to forecasting and budget projection initiatives as needed, supporting strategic planning with data-driven modeling.
Clearly communicate, document and present findings, methodologies, and model specifications for cross-functional, non-technical stakeholders.
Support data-driven decision-making by quantifying uncertainty, assessing statistical significance, and challenging assumptions when the data doesn't support them.
Identify and quantify relationships between behavioral signals, traffic sources, campaign attributes, and lead quality outcomes; distinguish meaningful correlations from spurious ones.
Serve as an analytical partner to media buyers and marketing teams: investigate campaign and source performance, validate hypotheses about lead quality, and provide statistically rigorous answers to business questions.


Requirements:


3+ years of experience in data science, machine learning, or a related quantitative role.
Comfortable working in agentic AI-assisted development environments (e.g., Cursor, ClaudeCode, Antigravity) to accelerate model development, data exploration, and pipeline building, including the judgment to know when AI-generated outputs need manual verification or refinement.
Strong proficiency in Python, including ML libraries such as scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, or similar.
Solid experience with feature engineering, particularly from behavioral, clickstream, or event-based data.
Familiarity with deploying models in production environments; experience with real-time or near-real-time inference is a strong plus.
Strong foundation in statistical inference, hypothesis testing and root-cause analysis: can distinguish signal from noise, assess significance, and communicate uncertainty clearly.
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large-scale datasets.
Understanding of classification, regression, and clustering techniques; experience with user segmentation or propensity modeling preferred.
Exposure to digital marketing concepts and KPIs (CPL, CVR, ROAS, etc.) is highly desirable.
Ability to translate vague business questions into testable hypotheses and structured analyses.
Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to work through ambiguity and define structure where none exists.
Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Team-oriented mindset with a collaborative and proactive approach.
Upper-Intermediate or higher level of English.


Nice to Have


Experience building or using custom skills, prompt workflows, or tool-use patterns within agentic coding environments.
Experience in lead generation, ad-tech, or performance marketing environments.
Familiarity with ad network optimization, postback mechanisms, or conversion APIs.
Experience with cloud platforms and MLOps tools.
Background in time-series forecasting or demand modeling.


What We Offer


Remote Work - we offer a truly and fully remote environment. You choose where you are the most productive and comfortable to have an impact.
Paid vacations - generous paid vacation policy to ensure you have time to recharge
Unlimited Sick Days - we understand that being only human means getting sick or feeling under the weather from time to time, so we guarantee you time off as long as you need to recover and get back on your feet.
Ongoing Learning - people at CapsLock are deeply inquisitive and eager to learn new knowledge and skills, that's why we support and create learning opportunities like the Free Books program, workshops, conferences, and more.
Home Office - we will cover the equipment and furniture expenses to make sure you have the best work-from-home experience.
Physical Well–Being - We will cover costs of your medical insurance. Additionally we offer an allowance for a flexible fitness program.
Fun Stuff - there is never a shortage of fun stuff

Title: Freelance Data Scraping Engineer (Python)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

About The Role

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets.
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements.
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior.
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery.
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $37 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
Daarom kies je voor AG




'Supporter van je leven' is niet zomaar een slogan. Het is een ambitie, een mindset. Want bij AG staan mensen centraal. Wij zorgen ervoor dat jouw job zin heeft, zodat jij zin hebt om bij ons te werken. Dag in dag uit maken we samen het verschil voor onze klanten. Zodat ze in alle rust van hun leven kunnen genieten.




Dit is jouw topjob




We zijn op zoek naar een getalenteerde AI Data Scientist met een zakelijke mindset. Je komt terecht in een dynamische omgeving die kansen biedt om analytische vaardigheden toe te passen bij het oplossen van bedrijfsproblemen in zowel België als de rest van de Ageas Groep in andere entiteiten.




Belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden:




Je ontwerpt, ontwikkelt en implementeert data- en AI-oplossingen voor zakelijke behoeften, aan de hand van zowel interne als externe gegevensbronnen.
Je identificeert en communiceert inzichten uit gegevensanalyse om besluitvorming en probleemoplossing te ondersteunen.
Je werkt samen met cross-functionele teams om problemen met gegevens te identificeren en op te lossen.
Je controleert voortdurend AI-modellen op nauwkeurigheid en identificeert en verhelpt proactief verschilen in gegevens.




Herken je jezelf hierin?




Je bezit een masterdiploma in een kwantitatief vakgebied (bijv. statistiek, wiskunde, informatica, economie, bedrijfskunde) of een verwant vakgebied met een sterke focus op gegevensanalyse.
Je hebt minstens vijf jaar ervaring met gegevensanalyse, bij voorkeur in een bedrijfsomgeving.
Je hebt ervaring met AI & GenAI, computervisie, NLP en/of deep learning.
Je hebt een sterke kennis van gegevensanalysetechnieken en -tools, zoals Python, SQL, PBI, DataBricks.
Je hebt een sterk zakelijk inzicht en je bent in staat om zakelijke behoeften en vereisten te begrijpen en te communiceren.
Je hebt uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, en kan complexe gegevens op een duidelijke en beknopte manier voorstellen. Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van het Nederlands, Frans en Engels.
Je kan zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband werken, meerdere prioriteiten beheren en strakke deadlines halen.
Je bent bereid om af en toe te reizen en projecten in andere entiteiten aan te nemen.




Dit zijn jouw voordelen




Je krijgt een loonpakket met heel wat voordelen. Een deel daarvan kan je zelf samenstellen. Een leasewagen, extra verlof of verzekeringen? Jij kiest.
Jouw welzijn staat voorop. Daarom zet AG in op een sportief en inspirerend welzijnsprogramma. Toegankelijk voor iedereen.
Flexibele werkuren? Thuiswerken tot drie dagen per week? Bij AG kan het.
Je krijgt de kans om jezelf voortdurend te ontwikkelen. Met een breed aanbod aan opleidingen leer je voortdurend vaardigheden bij. Zo geef je jezelf, en je carrière een boost.
Binnen AG veranderen van functie? En dan zo je comfortzone uitbreiden? Ook dat kan. Jouw carrière, jouw keuze.
Je komt terecht in een dynamische, duurzame werkomgeving. Waar de kantoren ruimte geven om slim samen te werken. En om je volledig te kunnen geven




Ga het avontuur aan




Helemaal verkocht? Druk op de knop en solliciteer vandaag nog. Heb je ondersteuning nodig voor of tijdens het selectieproces? We helpen je graag.

Show more

Title: Científico de datos
Description:
Senior freelance Data Scientist & Energy Optimization Engineer 🚀




A leading technology company is looking for someone who bridges data science with mathematical optimization to design the models that turn ML predictions into market-ready bids.




The challenge: make sure the full pipeline (forecasting + optimization) delivers reliable capacity to the grid. You define the bidding logic, model the physics of EV charging and shape how the company scales into new markets.




Key facts 📌

💼 Freelance via Shakers
📅 Duration: 12 months
⏱️ Full time
📍 100% remote (working-hours overlap 9:30 to 17:00 CET, ±2h)




What you'll do 🎯

Design and implement optimization models for EV fleet scheduling with Gurobi (or Pyomo)
Formulate bidding strategies for mFRR and FCR-D markets under uncertainty
Build the bridge between ML model outputs (energy forecasts, disconnect probabilities, price predictions) and optimizer inputs
Develop genetic-algorithm meta-optimization for fleet selection
Validate optimizer outputs against market requirements (bid fulfillment, endurance, activation readiness)
Model EV charging physics: energy envelopes, power curves, SoC constraints
Contribute to market strategy: quantify fleet flexibility, estimate K_red, feed pre-qualifications




What we need ✅

5+ years in mathematical optimization, operations research or quantitative energy systems
Strong proficiency with at least one of: Gurobi, Pyomo, CPLEX or OR-Tools
Formulating and solving MILPs, LPs or stochastic programs for scheduling/dispatch
Solid Python (pandas, NumPy; PySpark a plus)
Understanding of energy market mechanics (balancing, capacity, frequency regulation or demand response)
Translating physical constraints (power limits, energy conservation, time coupling) into mathematical formulations
Experience validating optimization outputs against real operational data




Nice to have ⭐

EV charging, V2G or distributed energy resource aggregation
Nordic energy markets (SvK mFRR/FCR-D, Nord Pool)
Genetic algorithms or metaheuristics (PyGAD or similar)
Probabilistic or robust optimization under forecast uncertainty
MLflow and Databricks/Spark




Why this project?

A technical leadership role where your models control real MW of flexibility on the grid, at a critical growth stage. Modern stack (Gurobi, Python, Databricks, Delta Lake, CI/CD), close collaboration with the optimization team and real weight in the market expansion strategy.




If this is you, apply and we'll review your profile 📩




#DataScience #Optimization #EnergyMarkets #OperationsResearch #Freelance #RemoteWorkork

Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer
Description:
About Devoteam:

Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer to join our growing Data & AI team (50 FTE) in Belgium. In this role, you will play a key part in designing, developing, and deploying machine learning solutions for our clients across various industries. You will collaborate with other data scientists, engineers, and consultants to deliver innovative and impactful solutions that address our clients' business challenges.

Responsibilities:


Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models and algorithms
Analyze large and complex datasets to identify patterns and insights
Build and maintain data pipelines for data ingestion, processing, and transformation
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into technical solutions
Stay up to date with the latest advancements in machine learning and artificial intelligence
Contribute to the development of Devoteam's data science and machine learning capabilities



Qualifications

Qualifications:


Master's degree or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
5+ years of experience in a data science or machine learning role
Strong programming skills in Python or R
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Experience with Databricks is a plus
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Fluent in English and proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory.
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Bonus Points:


Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark, or Hive
Experience with MLOps tools and practices
Experience with natural language processing or computer vision
Contributions to open-source projects or publications in relevant conferences or journals



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Data scientist
Description:
Hoe maak jij mee het verschil?

Als Data Scientist – in de Corporate strategy dienst – ontwikkel en onderhoud je strategische modellen die diepgaande inzichten opleveren en direct bijdragen aan beleidskeuzes en investeringsbeslissingen.

Je werkt op het kruispunt van data, business en strategie en zorgt ervoor dat complexe assumpties en berekeningen transparant, robuust en reproduceerbaar worden geïmplementeerd in schaalbare data‑oplossingen.

Wat is jouw rol bij NMBS?

Als Data Scientist heb je de volgende verantwoordelijkheden:


Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en industrialiseert strategische modellen op basis van businessassumpties, in samenwerking met de relevante stakeholders.
Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende NMBS-directies in een transversale context over de hele organisatie.
Je vertaalt bestaande Excel‑modellen, die vandaag lokaal beheerd worden, naar robuuste en schaalbare PySpark‑oplossingen.
Je automatiseert dataverwerking en modelruns via pipelines in Databricks.
Je werkt met Python en PySpark binnen een Azure Databricks‑omgeving om data te verwerken, modellen te implementeren en analyses uit te voeren.
Je waakt over transparantie, reproduceerbaarheid en best practices bij het implementeren van modellen.
Je verkent opportuniteiten om bijkomende databronnen en analysetechnieken (zoals ruimtelijke data, AI of geavanceerde visualisaties) te integreren wanneer deze een duidelijke meerwaarde bieden voor strategische inzichten.
Je volgt de AI en machine learning evoluties actief op en denkt mee na over hoe ze het strategisch modelleren kunnen versterken.


Zo werken we


Ontwikkeling gebeurt lokaal in VS Code in een gedeelde Git‑repository.
Samenwerking verloopt via pull requests in Azure DevOps.
Deployment en uitvoering gebeuren in Azure Databricks.
We werken volgens een agile manier van werken, met iteratieve ontwikkeling, korte feedbackcycli en nauwe samenwerking met stakeholders.
We volgen best practices rond codekwaliteit, versiebeheer en samenwerking.
Je werkt in een team waar kennisdeling, transparantie en kwaliteit centraal staan.


Waarom is deze job iets voor jou?


Je beschikt over een masterdiploma bij voorkeur in wiskunde, statistiek, data science, ingenieurswetenschappen of een andere kwantitatieve richting) (of gelijkwaardig door ervaring) EN een ervaring van minstens 4 jaar ervaring in de industrie als data scientist.


EN je beschikt over de volgende vaardigheden:


Je hebt een sterke Python‑kennis voor data wrangling data‑analyse en modellering.
Je hebt ervaring met PySpark en het werken met grotere datasets.
Je hebt ervaring met het industrialiseren van analyses of modellen (bijvoorbeeld migratie van Excel naar productiecode).
Je communiceert vlot, zowel in het Nederlands als in het Engels en je kan complexe modellen en assumpties helder toelichten aan niet‑technische stakeholders.
Je hebt een gestructureerde mindset en bent oplossings- en kwaliteitsgericht.
Heb je interesse in machine learning, AI-toepassingen, geografische data of GIS-tools, transport, mobiliteit of complexe operationele systemen? Die zijn extra troeven.


Indien je jouw diploma in het buitenland hebt behaald, dien je in het bezit te zijn van een gelijkwaardigheidsattest afgeleverd door de Vlaamse Overheid (NARIC-Vlaanderen) om deel te kunnen nemen aan de selectie.

We vinden het dan ook belangrijk dat je jezelf kan zijn. Want dat maakt NMBS sterker. Daarom hechten we veel belang aan diversiteit en inclusie .

Wat mag je van ons verwachten?

Kiezen voor NMBS is kiezen voor een omgeving waarin je kan bijleren en groeien. Je wordt gesteund en omringd door fijne collega's. Als Data Scientist krijg je een loonpakket dat bestaat uit:


Een aantrekkelijk salaris aangevuld met een 13e maand, onkostenvergoeding, maaltijdcheques en een collectieve en individuele bonus.
Een deel van het salaris wordt aangeboden als een flexibel budget. Je kan daarbij kiezen voor fietslease, lease auto, terugbetaling pensioensparen, ...
Een hospitalisatie- en groepsverzekering.
Flexibele werkuren, de mogelijkheid om te telewerken en 33 vakantiedagen per jaar.
Een treinabonnement in België, kortingen op internationale treinreizen en een fietsvergoeding.
Voordelen voor je gezin: o.a. kortingen op gezinsactiviteiten en in winkelketens.


Waar ga je werken?

Je werkt in de buurt van het station Brussel-Zuid, in het gebouw Delta.

What’s next?


Je solliciteert online.
We screenen je CV en motivatiebrief. We bellen je eventueel op om kennis te maken en om je kennis van het Frans te bevragen.
We nodigen je uit voor een eerst (online) sollicitatiegesprek waarin je je motivatie en competenties kan tonen. Ter voorbereiding op dit gesprek vragen we jou om een online persoonlijkheidsvragenlijst in te vullen. Ter plaatse, bereid je een business case voor.
Verloopt alles positief? Volgt er nog een tweede gesprek.
We doen je een contractvoorstel.
Je start in je nieuwe job!


Wij behouden ons het recht om deze procedure aan te passen of op te schorten indien nodig. Ons rekruteringsteam zal de profielen selecteren die het best aansluiten bij de functie-eisen om een optimale match te garanderen.

Hoe maak jij mee het verschil?

Als Data Scientist – in de Corporate strategy dienst – ontwikkel en onderhoud je strategische modellen die diepgaande inzichten opleveren en direct bijdragen aan beleidskeuzes en investeringsbeslissingen.

Je werkt op het kruispunt van data, business en strategie en zorgt ervoor dat complexe assumpties en berekeningen transparant, robuust en reproduceerbaar worden geïmplementeerd in schaalbare data‑oplossingen.

Wat is jouw rol bij NMBS?

Als Data Scientist heb je de volgende verantwoordelijkheden:

  • Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en industrialiseert strategische modellen op basis van businessassumpties, in samenwerking met de relevante stakeholders.
  • Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende NMBS-directies in een transversale context over de hele organisatie.
  • Je vertaalt bestaande Excel‑modellen, die vandaag lokaal beheerd worden, naar robuuste en schaalbare PySpark‑oplossingen.
  • Je automatiseert dataverwerking en modelruns via pipelines in Databricks.
  • Je werkt met Python en PySpark binnen een Azure Databricks‑omgeving om data te verwerken, modellen te implementeren en analyses uit te voeren.
  • Je waakt over transparantie, reproduceerbaarheid en best practices bij het implementeren van modellen.
  • Je verkent opportuniteiten om bijkomende databronnen en analysetechnieken (zoals ruimtelijke data, AI of geavanceerde visualisaties) te integreren wanneer deze een duidelijke meerwaarde bieden voor strategische inzichten.
  • Je volgt de AI en machine learning evoluties actief op en denkt mee na over hoe ze het strategisch modelleren kunnen versterken.

Zo werken we

  • Ontwikkeling gebeurt lokaal in VS Code in een gedeelde Git‑repository.
  • Samenwerking verloopt via pull requests in Azure DevOps.
  • Deployment en uitvoering gebeuren in Azure Databricks.
  • We werken volgens een agile manier van werken, met iteratieve ontwikkeling, korte feedbackcycli en nauwe samenwerking met stakeholders.
  • We volgen best practices rond codekwaliteit, versiebeheer en samenwerking.
  • Je werkt in een team waar kennisdeling, transparantie en kwaliteit centraal staan.

Waarom is deze job iets voor jou?

  • Je beschikt over een masterdiploma bij voorkeur in wiskunde, statistiek, data science, ingenieurswetenschappen of een andere kwantitatieve richting) (of gelijkwaardig door ervaring) EN een ervaring van minstens 4 jaar ervaring in de industrie als data scientist.

EN je beschikt over de volgende vaardigheden:

  • Je hebt een sterke Python‑kennis voor data wrangling data‑analyse en modellering.
  • Je hebt ervaring met PySpark en het werken met grotere datasets.
  • Je hebt ervaring met het industrialiseren van analyses of modellen (bijvoorbeeld migratie van Excel naar productiecode).
  • Je communiceert vlot, zowel in het Nederlands als in het Engels en je kan complexe modellen en assumpties helder toelichten aan niet‑technische stakeholders.
  • Je hebt een gestructureerde mindset en bent oplossings- en kwaliteitsgericht.
  • Heb je interesse in machine learning, AI-toepassingen, geografische data of GIS-tools, transport, mobiliteit of complexe operationele systemen? Die zijn extra troeven.

Indien je jouw diploma in het buitenland hebt behaald, dien je in het bezit te zijn van een gelijkwaardigheidsattest afgeleverd door de Vlaamse Overheid (NARIC-Vlaanderen) om deel te kunnen nemen aan de selectie.

We vinden het dan ook belangrijk dat je jezelf kan zijn. Want dat maakt NMBS sterker. Daarom hechten we veel belang aan diversiteit en inclusie .

Wat mag je van ons verwachten?

Kiezen voor NMBS is kiezen voor een omgeving waarin je kan bijleren en groeien. Je wordt gesteund en omringd door fijne collega's. Als Data Scientist krijg je een loonpakket dat bestaat uit:

  • Een aantrekkelijk salaris aangevuld met een 13e maand, onkostenvergoeding, maaltijdcheques en een collectieve en individuele bonus.
  • Een deel van het salaris wordt aangeboden als een flexibel budget. Je kan daarbij kiezen voor fietslease, lease auto, terugbetaling pensioensparen, ...
  • Een hospitalisatie- en groepsverzekering.
  • Flexibele werkuren, de mogelijkheid om te telewerken en 33 vakantiedagen per jaar.
  • Een treinabonnement in België, kortingen op internationale treinreizen en een fietsvergoeding.
  • Voordelen voor je gezin: o.a. kortingen op gezinsactiviteiten en in winkelketens.

Waar ga je werken?

Je werkt in de buurt van het station Brussel-Zuid, in het gebouw Delta.

What’s next?

  • Je solliciteert online.
  • We screenen je CV en motivatiebrief. We bellen je eventueel op om kennis te maken en om je kennis van het Frans te bevragen.
  • We nodigen je uit voor een eerst (online) sollicitatiegesprek waarin je je motivatie en competenties kan tonen. Ter voorbereiding op dit gesprek vragen we jou om een online persoonlijkheidsvragenlijst in te vullen. Ter plaatse, bereid je een business case voor.
  • Verloopt alles positief? Volgt er nog een tweede gesprek.
  • We doen je een contractvoorstel.
  • Je start in je nieuwe job!

Wij behouden ons het recht om deze procedure aan te passen of op te schorten indien nodig. Ons rekruteringsteam zal de profielen selecteren die het best aansluiten bij de functie-eisen om een optimale match te garanderen.

Heb je nog vragen?

Title: Data & AI Science Consultant
Description:
Are you willing to make a step in your career and deliver Analytics or AI products from early discovery stages up to industrialized solutions to re-imagine the future of the industry as part of large transformation projects?

If this is the case, this role will be of interest to you!

Team



Be at the forefront of the industry, you’ll create, own and make it a reality for clients looking to better serve their connected customers and operate always-on enterprises.

Become an integral part of our Data& AI team with the credibility, expertise and insight clients depend on.

Be working with famous brands and household names – no worrying about how to explain what you do to your family again!

Work alongside global teams to collaborate and deliver the best solutions for our clients.

Align with our industry partners to develop your career and the solutions we deliver for our clients.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Process



Drive and implement real world solutions into production that drive business value to our clients

Solve challenging business problems using advanced machine learning methods such as Deep Learning and quantitative analytics.

Understand business requirements and support the development of business cases.

Run discovery analytics to identify new and innovative opportunities.

Partner with developers and engineers to deploy machine learning algorithms to deliver business value.

Define approaches to embed and scale machine learning models.

Build reusable assets, solutions and develop best practices for current and future business problems.

Consult on complex analyses and advanced machine learning methods.

Communicate and provide guidance to senior client leadership and teams.



Data



Contribute data science expertise to new sales activities.

Shape DS Strategy and the delivery of DS solutions.

Operate across multiple industries and functions.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Your team

The Data and AI revolution is changing everything. It’s everywhere – transforming how we work and play. Join Accenture and help transform leading organisations and communities around the world. Accenture Data and AI Science is driving these exciting changes and bringing them to life across 40 industries in more than 120 countries. The sheer scale of our capabilities and client engagements and the way we collaborate with the ecosystem, operate and deliver value provides an unparalleled opportunity to grow and advance.

Accenture’s Data and AI practice covers the range of Data and AI skills, from Strategy, Data Science, Data Architecture, AI Engineering and Visual Insights. When combined with Accenture’s broader Strategy and Consulting practice, we are able to bring together the unique ability to drive end to end business change through the application of Data and AI.

You'll learn, grow and advance in an innovative culture that thrives on shared success, diverse ways of thinking and enables boundaryless opportunities that can drive your career in new and exciting ways.

If you’re looking for a challenging career working in a vibrant environment with access to training and a global network of experts, this could be the role for you. As part of our global team, you'll be working with cutting-edge technologies and will have the opportunity to develop a wide range of new skills on the job.

Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services. With our thought leadership and culture of innovation, we apply industry expertise, diverse skill sets and next-generation technology to each business challenge.

We believe in inclusion and diversity and supporting the whole person. Our core values comprise of Stewardship, Best People, Client Value Creation, One Global Network, Respect for the Individual and Integrity. Year after year, Accenture is recognized worldwide not just for business performance but for inclusion and diversity too.

“Across the globe, one thing is universally true of the people of Accenture: We care deeply about what we do and the impact we have with our clients and with the communities in which we work and live. It is personal to all of us.” – Julie Sweet, Accenture CEO

Your background



Relevant work experience in data science, machine learning, and business analytics

Practical experience in coding languages eg. Python, R, Scala, etc.; (Python preferred)

Proficiency in database technologies eg. SQL, ETL, No-SQL, DW, and Big Data technologies e.g. pySpark, Hive, etc.

Experienced working with structured and also unstructured data eg. Text, PDFs, jpgs, call recordings, video, etc.

Knowledge of machine learning modelling techniques and how to fine-tune those models eg. XGBoost, Deep Neural Networks, Transformers, Markov chains, etc.

Experience using specialized machine learning libraries e.g. Fastai, Keras, Tensorflow, pytorch, sci-kit learn, huggingface, etc.

Must demonstrate the capacity of reading, understanding and implementing new techniques in the field of machine learning as they emerge.

Experience of using Cloud technologies eg. AWS, GCP or Azure.

Specialised visualisation techniques eg. D3.js, ggplot etc.

Strong verbal/written communication & data presentation skills

Awareness of Large Language Models (LLMs) and a strong enthusiasm for continuous learning and staying updated with the latest advancements in the field

Fluent in English, in Dutch and/or in French



Bonus point If you…



Willingness and ability to learn quickly and apply creative thinking to finding great solutions and drive their implementation

Ability to successfully build relationships with clients and stakeholders

Mastery of problem solving and solutioning

Proven history and background in statistics/mathematics/macroeconomics



Our offer

Accenture is an incredible place to work - and keep learning. By joining us, you’ll become part of a global company with a world-class brand and reputation. Besides the work we do for our clients, we’re really proud of our vibrant, diverse workplace culture: we believe in openness and honesty, fairness and equality, common sense and realism. We want to get to know the real you and help you explore and grow - whatever it is you're great at. So you will always have lots of learning opportunities (formal and informal) to improve your role-specific skills and expertise.

Besides our high-profile, challenging projects and our nurturing work environment, we offer excellent employee benefits, including:



13th month + holiday allowance

20 + 12 holidays

Laptop and smartphone

Hospitalization insurance and extensive group insurance package

Bonus program

Training & learning opportunities

Green mobility program: e-bikes, public transport, bike 2 work allowance

Flex-rewards: decide on your rewards package with our flexible benefits tool

Discount program: get discounts at your favorite (online) shops

Employee shares purchase plan

Eco-cheques

And many more!

Title: Python(Machine leaning) QA Lead - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Python(Machine learning) Quality Assurance Lead

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

About This Role

In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as a Python(Machine learning) Quality Assurance Lead to oversee quality, consistency, and trainer performance across Python machine learning AI training projects. You will review AI-generated Python code, ML workflows, model explanations, and trainer/QA work; evaluate output quality against project guidelines; provide precise written feedback; and ensure contributors follow expected quality standards. You will assess work for code correctness, machine learning methodology, statistical validity, reproducibility, model-evaluation quality, data leakage risks, package usage, debugging accuracy, readability, maintainability, formatting, instruction-following, and adherence to project-specific rubrics. This role requires strong Python and ML expertise, English communication skills, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to manage quality workflows across remote technical teams. This role is a fast-growing AI Data Services company delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs. Your Python ML quality leadership will help ensure training data is accurate, executable, statistically sound, reproducible, clearly explained, and aligned with client expectations. Selection process involves an AI interview, a domain-specific task, and an interview with a recruiter. Important: There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.

Your Profile


Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a closely related quantitative field.
Strong grasp of English to follow guidelines, communicate with teams, and provide clear technical feedback.
3+ years of professional experience in Python development, machine learning, data science, ML engineering, model evaluation, research engineering, technical review, or ML education.
Strong understanding of Python fundamentals such as data structures, functions, classes, iterators, comprehensions, exception handling, virtual environments, package management, testing, and debugging.
Strong understanding of ML topics such as supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, train/test splits, cross-validation, model selection, data leakage, regression, classification, clustering, metrics, bias/variance, regularization, and reproducibility.
Ability to evaluate ML content against detailed rubrics and identify issues such as flawed methodology, wrong metrics, data leakage, non-reproducible code, invalid assumptions, hallucinated APIs, misleading conclusions, or incomplete explanations.
Familiarity with NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, XGBoost/LightGBM, Jupyter, matplotlib, seaborn, MLflow, Hugging Face, SQL, GitHub, Docker, and CI/CD is preferred.
Experience leading or supporting remote teams of trainers, annotators, reviewers, engineers, data scientists, ML researchers, coding mentors, or QAs is strongly preferred.
Comfortable working in fast-moving remote environments using Discord, Google Sheets, Google Docs, trackers, dashboards, GitHub, and project management systems.
Highly organized and able to maintain style guides, trackers, FAQs, onboarding materials, honeypots, calibration tasks, and quality documentation.
Experience with AI training, data annotation, LLM evaluation, code QA, ML QA, or rubric-based technical review is a strong plus.


Key Responsibilities


Quality monitoring: Spot-check Python ML items, identify quality issues, provide feedback through DMs, and escalate recurring or critical issues.
Code and ML review: Evaluate AI-generated Python code, ML pipelines, data-preprocessing steps, model training workflows, evaluation logic, debugging responses, and explanations for correctness and reproducibility.
Trainer and QA communication: Update contributors on Discord about guideline changes, workflow updates, and Python/ML-specific review standards.
Question handling: Respond to questions around Python syntax, package usage, data leakage, model validation, metrics, statistical assumptions, reproducibility, notebooks, and rubric interpretation.
Trainer/QA activation management: DM inactive contributors, encourage activation, track follow-ups, and flag availability issues.
Documentation: Create and maintain Python ML style guides, trackers, FAQs, examples, honeypots, calibration tasks, and onboarding materials.
Onboarding and training: Run onboarding/training calls for Python ML contributors.
Risk review: Flag misleading, overconfident, statistically invalid, non-reproducible, insecure, or non-production-ready Python ML recommendations.
Process improvement: Identify recurring quality gaps and build scalable QA processes.

Title: Business Banking Data Scientist
Description:
A day in the life of a Data Scientist III:


The Data Scientist will closely monitor and contribute to the development and execution of a key project in collaboration with external consultants, ensuring sufficient knowledge transfer and readiness to assume full ownership upon project delivery completion.
Trying different approaches until you succeed if the problem is hard to crack or a technical solution hard to introduce.
Making original solutions work in real-life by looking at things from a different perspective. Next, to being creative, you are also persistent.
Working in cross-functional teams with ambitious goals, asking fellow team members for assistance, but also helping them out by sharing your knowledge and capabilities.
Collaborating with many different parties throughout our organization enabled by your solid communication skills.
Mobilizing people to capture the tremendous value that Data Science brings to customers of ING via your enthusiasm and passion that are contagious,


How To Succeed

We hire smart people like you for your potential. Our biggest expectation is that you’ll stay curious. Keep learning. Take on responsibility. In return, we’ll back you to develop into an even more awesome version of yourself.


Machine learning, AI & data science knowledge to be applied on impactful projects.
You have at least 5 years of relevant applied experience
MSc or Ph.D. with excellent academic results in the field of Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or other quantitative fields
Analytical and content strength (in DS work field): sees which DS techniques apply to the business problem at hand, able to structure the approach and a good programmer.
Technical efficiency to create production ready models and roll them out.
Knowledge of Python, Spark, SQL; GCP & other cloud providers, GIT, ML algorithms, Model lifecycle.
Stakeholder management and strong communication skills.
Financial and banking knowledge as well as how it translates to analytic


The Team

An informal, dynamic environment with innovative colleagues supporting your endeavours.

We work in a progressive and agile way of working, where new ideas are valued ahead of

convention. The team is composed of Data Analysts that has to be supported by a Data Scientist (to be hired). You will be joining us on our project-based way of working where most tracks are worked on by a small, dedicated team of 2-3 people mixing the roles available in the team with extra support from subject matter experts specific to the track. This project-based way of working also means you will have the possibility to work on different topics ranging from pure marketing ‘propensity to buy’, to more foundational building blocks of the bank, such as classifying customer feedbacks all the way to customer facing features of the app like transaction categorization.

Would you like to get to know the department better? Book a coffee with the hiring manager, a team member, or the recruiter. In a second phase you could get to know the job content better via a My Job Your Job .

Title: Data Scientist - GenAI
Description:
Join Artefact Belgium: Shape the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Are you an early-career professional excited to harness the power of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is seeking a Data Scientist - Gen AI to join our innovative team in Brussels. Our Belgian team specializes in LLM and GenAI projects, and this is your chance to work on cutting-edge solutions, collaborate with top-tier experts, and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic, forward-thinking environment.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Motivated to apply your technical expertise to real-world challenges in AI. Able to thrive in collaborative settings while independently driving your own workstreams. Eager to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible in AI innovation.

Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. At Artefact, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, with a strong commitment to inclusivity and employee growth.

What You Will Do


Collaborate with the team to design, train, and fine-tune state-of-the-art models for client projects and internal tools.
Develop and integrate agentic AI solutions using tool calling, and emerging protocols like Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create autonomous, context-aware applications.
Research and implement innovative generative AI applications to solve complex business problems.
Design and implement comprehensive evaluation frameworks to measure LLM performance, establishing quality metrics and benchmarks that guide development decisions and ensure reliable, production-ready solutions.
Optimize the performance, scalability, and usability of LLM-powered products and tools.
Contribute to the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including models, APIs, and supporting infrastructure.
Participate in technical discussions and brainstorming sessions to shape impactful solutions.


What You Bring


A degree in computer science, mathematics, engineering, physics, or a related field.
Experience working with LLMs, generative AI, or advanced NLP applications (e.g., fine-tuning models, using frameworks like Hugging Face or OpenAI).
Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with AI and NLP libraries.
Analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with an ability to address real-world challenges through AI.
Good communication skills in English, Dutch or French; additional languages are a plus.
A growth mindset, adaptability, and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment.


What We Offer


A competitive salary with benefits tailored for early-career professionals.
Hands-on experience with state-of-the-art LLM technologies in impactful projects.
A dynamic and supportive work environment that fosters creativity and professional development.
Resources for ongoing training, certification, and skill-building in generative AI and related fields.
A collaborative, inclusive workplace with access to global expertise through Artefact's network.


This position is based in Brussels.

Don't worry if you don't meet every criterion! We value passion, potential, and a commitment to innovation. If you're excited about generative AI and ready to grow your career, we'd love to hear from you!

Title: Python Developer
Description:
Python / API Developer | Python, AWS, FastAPI, 2D / 3D Images

Rate - €90–€95 per hour

Location - Erlangen, Germany

The Working Pattern - Flexible, with frequent onsite visits required

Duration - Long-term




About the Client

Digital Skills are working with a global sportswear brand on an innovative AI programme focused on transforming digital product design. The initiative centres on leveraging machine learning and 3D technologies to enhance how internal product (fashion and apparel) teams create, process and interact with product data.




About the Role

This is a backend focused Python development role supporting a cutting-edge AI platform used by designers and engineers. You will primarily build and maintain APIs that expose AI-driven functionality, with a strong emphasis on working with image based and 3D data used across product design workflows.




Responsibilities

Develop and maintain backend APIs that expose AI and machine learning capabilities to internal users
Build RESTful services using Python frameworks such as FastAPI
Integrate APIs with AWS based infrastructure, including compute and storage services
Work with 2D and 3D data formats, including images, segmentation outputs, textures, and 3D assets such as meshes and Blender files
Support data preparation and validation pipelines for multimodal datasets (image and 3D data)
Design API endpoints that process image inputs and return structured outputs such as masks or derived assets
Collaborate with AI engineers and product teams to support model integration and usage
Ensure backend services are scalable, secure and performant




Desired Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience as a Python backend developer with a focus on API development
Proven experience building REST APIs, ideally using FastAPI or similar frameworks
Working knowledge of AWS services such as S3 and EC2
Practical experience handling non-tabular data, particularly images and/or 3D assets
Exposure to Computer Vision or Computer Graphics concepts, such as image processing, segmentation, or working with visual data pipelines
Familiarity with 3D data formats, rendering pipelines, or tools such as Blender is advantageous
Experience running or integrating machine learning inference workflows (e.g. PyTorch)
Working knowledge of SQL and backend data integration
Strong software engineering fundamentals, with a Computer Science or similar background




Summary of the Best Candidate

The ideal candidate is a strong backend Python engineer with proven API development experience and a solid software engineering foundation. You will not be a data scientist, but you must be comfortable working with visual data, including both 2D images and 3D assets, and understand how these differ from traditional datasets.




You will have several years of commercial development experience and be confident building robust backend systems that interact with AI driven processes. Exposure to Computer Vision or 3D data handling will set you apart, particularly where you have worked with image pipelines, segmentation, or asset-based workflows.




This role suits someone who can independently deliver high quality backend services while collaborating closely with teams working at the intersection of AI, design, and engineering.

Title: Data Scientist - Banking
Description:
Data Scientist – Financial Services

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international organization headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with over 350 consultants dedicated to driving business transformation through innovative technology.

Our Financial Services (FS) practice includes 100+ specialists helping banks and financial institutions adapt to evolving client expectations, leverage cutting-edge technologies, and navigate increasingly complex regulatory environments.

At NORRIQ, we believe that people are our greatest asset. We foster a culture of innovation, growth, and excellence, offering tailored training, clear career paths, and a strong sense of community. Join us on this exciting journey and become a valued member of the NORRIQ family — connected for life!

The Opportunity

NORRIQ Financial Services is looking for a Data Scientist to accelerate our data analytics, machine learning, and generative AI initiatives. You will work with leading financial institutions to design, build, and deliver data-driven and AI-powered solutions that create tangible business impact.

Roles & Responsibilities

Client-Facing Responsibilities


Design and implement machine learning and generative AI solutions for complex financial use cases.
Conduct exploratory data analysis and build insightful dashboards or visualizations.
Present analytical findings and actionable recommendations to senior management and stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Data Engineers, Business Analysts, Developers) to industrialize AI solutions.
Provide technical guidance and advisory on data science and AI best practices.


Technical Responsibilities


Develop and maintain AI and ML models using Python, scikit-learn, and deep learning frameworks.
Apply a wide range of analytical methodologies, including:
Generative AI techniques (Prompt Engineering, RAG, Fine-tuning, Speech-to-Text, Agentic Applications, Image Generation & Interpretation)
Predictive AI and classical machine learning approaches.
Contribute to production-grade AI applications using tools such as GitLab, DVC, and MLOps frameworks.
Ensure reproducibility, version control, and performance optimization across models and pipelines.
Create and maintain Jupyter notebooks for experimentation and documentation.

Internal Contributions


Contribute to NORRIQ’s AI & Data Science Center of Excellence through knowledge sharing and internal projects.
Support proposal writing and solution architecture for client engagements.
Stay at the forefront of developments in data science, AI, and GenAI frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, etc.).


Our Data Scientist Must Have


Fluency in English, and Dutch or French, with at least passive knowledge of the other
Belgian residency
At least few years of experience in a similar role.


What NORRIQ Offers You


Be part of a fast-growing, international organization with a unique and people-centered culture
Enjoy a highly competitive compensation package, including a company car, iPhone, and additional allowances
Access extensive training programs in Belgium and Denmark, focusing on both technical expertise and soft skills
Work with a dynamic and diverse team of experienced professionals and young talent in a collaborative environment within the financial services sector


Join NORRIQ to elevate your career, contribute to transformative projects, and make a lasting impact in the financial services industry!

Title: AI Specialist (Remote)
Description:
Role: AI Specialist (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $22 - $70/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an AI Evaluation Specialist to work on a contract basis. The role involves evaluating AI models and datasets to ensure accuracy, reliability, and performance across different use cases. This includes analyzing model outputs, identifying biases, and validating results against defined benchmarks.




Key Responsibilities:

• Conduct structured evaluations of AI models and datasets to measure performance against predefined metrics.

• Identify and document biases, errors, or inconsistencies in AI outputs and recommend improvements.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to refine evaluation criteria and align them with business objectives.

• Develop and maintain standardized evaluation frameworks for consistent assessment of AI systems.

• Generate detailed reports summarizing evaluation findings, trends, and actionable recommendations.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Experience with AI model evaluation, testing, and validation in a technical or research capacity.

• Proficiency in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar tools.

• Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and derive meaningful insights.

• Familiarity with bias detection, fairness metrics, and ethical considerations in AI systems.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills to document findings and present recommendations.

• Ability to work independently in a remote environment with minimal supervision.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the technology industry, contributing to the development of trustworthy and high-performing AI systems. Candidates will have the chance to shape evaluation standards that impact real-world applications.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

Title: Data / AI Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer – Analytics & AI Platform (EU Institution via Unisys)




Role Overview




We are looking for a talented Data Engineer to contribute to the design, development and deployment of core platform components supporting data engineering, analytics and AI-enabled capabilities.

The role requires strong expertise in working with both structured and unstructured data, enabling advanced reporting, analytics and AI-driven use cases within a secure and highly governed environment.




Key Responsibilities




Data Engineering & Integration

Develop and maintain data pipelines integrating multiple heterogeneous data sources.
Implement batch and near-real-time data ingestion processes.
Perform data cleansing, validation and standardisation.
Apply metadata tagging and ensure data lineage.
Contribute to the maintenance and evolution of the Data & Product Catalogue.

Structured & Unstructured Data Management

Design solutions combining structured and unstructured data sources.
Transform document-centric and text-based data into analysis-ready formats.
Enable unified reporting and analytical workflows across different data types.
Support AI-driven processing of documents and other unstructured information.

Analytics & AI Enablement

Implement analytics capabilities supporting decision-making processes.
Contribute to AI-enabled use cases involving advanced data exploitation.
Ensure explainability, traceability and human oversight of AI-generated outputs.

Automation & Orchestration

Automate data pipelines and reporting workflows.
Implement event-driven processes, alerts and triggers.
Support monitoring, logging and operational observability across platform components.

Security & Compliance

Implement identity and access management controls.
Implement encryption for data at rest and in transit.
Implement audit logging capabilities.
Support the separation of secure and non-secure environments.
Contribute to solutions deployable in highly secure or isolated environments.

Platform Development & Integration

Develop scalable and modular platform components using open standards and APIs.
Support integration with existing enterprise systems and external data sources.
Contribute to hybrid and sovereign deployment approaches.

Collaboration & Delivery

Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders, including data governance teams, product owners and data stewards.
Contribute to use case implementation and end-to-end validation activities.
Produce technical documentation and support knowledge transfer initiatives.




Profile Requirements




Mandatory Experience & Skills

Proven experience designing and implementing data pipelines (ETL/ELT).
Strong knowledge of data lake and data warehouse architectures.
Experience with modern data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure or open-source data platforms.
Demonstrated experience handling and integrating structured and unstructured data.
Experience building end-to-end data pipelines across heterogeneous data sources.
Experience preparing data for reporting, analytics and AI use cases.
Ability to structure unstructured information using metadata, classification and transformation techniques.
Experience with analytics development and data modelling.
Exposure to AI and Machine Learning solutions, particularly involving text or document-based data.
Understanding of explainability and traceability principles.
Knowledge of DevOps, CI/CD and pipeline automation.
Ability to work effectively in complex multi-stakeholder environments.

Nice-to-Have Skills

Experience working in highly secure or restricted environments.
Familiarity with Microsoft Purview or equivalent data governance solutions.
Experience with data catalogues and business glossaries.
Experience in cross-domain data management.
Experience in hybrid or sovereign cloud environments.
Experience within public sector or international organisations.
Experience with AI explainability or human-in-the-loop systems.




Skills & Competencies




Strong engineering mindset with a focus on scalable and modular solutions.
Ability to operate in highly governed and security-sensitive environments.
Pragmatic and structured approach focused on value delivery and feasibility.
Adaptability to changing priorities and technologies.
Strong collaboration and communication skills.
Ability to work effectively in multidisciplinary teams.




👉 Please note: Only candidates who are nationals of one of the EU Member States are eligible to apply for this role.




Sounds exciting and want to know more?




Apply directly or contact Goffinet François.

  • Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders, including data governance teams, product owners and data stewards.
  • Contribute to use case implementation and end-to-end validation activities.
  • Produce technical documentation and support knowledge transfer initiatives.


Profile Requirements


Mandatory Experience & Skills

  • Proven experience designing and implementing data pipelines (ETL/ELT).
  • Strong knowledge of data lake and data warehouse architectures.
  • Experience with modern data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure or open-source data platforms.
  • Demonstrated experience handling and integrating structured and unstructured data.
  • Experience building end-to-end data pipelines across heterogeneous data sources.
  • Experience preparing data for reporting, analytics and AI use cases.
  • Ability to structure unstructured information using metadata, classification and transformation techniques.
  • Experience with analytics development and data modelling.
  • Exposure to AI and Machine Learning solutions, particularly involving text or document-based data.
  • Understanding of explainability and traceability principles.
  • Knowledge of DevOps, CI/CD and pipeline automation.
  • Ability to work effectively in complex multi-stakeholder environments.

Nice-to-Have Skills

  • Experience working in highly secure or restricted environments.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Purview or equivalent data governance solutions.
  • Experience with data catalogues and business glossaries.
  • Experience in cross-domain data management.
  • Experience in hybrid or sovereign cloud environments.
  • Experience within public sector or international organisations.
  • Experience with AI explainability or human-in-the-loop systems.


Skills & Competencies


  • Strong engineering mindset with a focus on scalable and modular solutions.
  • Ability to operate in highly governed and security-sensitive environments.
  • Pragmatic and structured approach focused on value delivery and feasibility.
  • Adaptability to changing priorities and technologies.
  • Strong collaboration and communication skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in multidisciplinary teams.


👉 Please note: Only candidates who are nationals of one of the EU Member States are eligible to apply for this role.


Sounds exciting and want to know more?


Apply directly or contact Goffinet François.

Title: Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer (Freelance)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Senior Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities:


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Requirements:


At least 5+ years of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required)
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Candidates should have a strong technical foundation and practical experience with scripting, automation, and AI-assisted workflows. We are looking for specialists who can solve non-trivial problems, work confidently with LLMs, and systematically collect, structure, and validate data from diverse sources. A methodical, detail-oriented approach and the ability to work independently are essential
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Demonstrated experience handling anti-bot mechanisms and dynamic site structures at scale
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or equivalent) and containerization (Docker) as part of real workflows
Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, OpenRouter, or similar) applied to automation tasks
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $45 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Python Developer / Data Engineer – MLOps
Description:
About the Opportunity




As part of the development of innovative solutions for the renewable energy sector, we are looking for a Python Developer / Data Engineer with an MLOps mindset to join a team responsible for deploying and maintaining Machine Learning models in production.

You will contribute to building solutions that forecast energy production from renewable assets (wind farms and photovoltaic plants), working closely with Data Scientists and business experts.




Your Responsibilities

Develop and maintain robust, scalable Python applications.
Design, build, and optimize Machine Learning training and deployment pipelines.
Prepare and transform data for Machine Learning models (feature engineering).
Automate model training, validation, and inference workflows.
Collaborate closely with Data Scientists and technical teams to improve the Machine Learning lifecycle.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of MLOps practices and data platform architecture.




Your Profile

You have solid experience in Python development and are eager to work in a Data Engineering and Machine Learning environment.




Required Skills

Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines.
Good understanding of MLOps concepts, including:
Feature engineering
Model training and hyperparameter tuning
Model deployment
Inference
Model versioning
Hands-on experience with the AWS ecosystem, including:
Amazon S3
AWS Step Functions
Amazon Athena




Nice to Have

Experience with any of the following technologies would be a plus:

Databricks
MLflow (Model Registry)
Feature Stores
Docker
Git
CI/CD




Soft Skills

Strong team player with excellent collaboration skills.
Passion for Python development and software engineering best practices.
Quality-oriented mindset with attention to clean, maintainable code.
Curious, proactive, and capable of solving complex technical challenges.




Technical Environment

Python
AWS (S3, Athena, Step Functions)
Machine Learning
Data Engineering
MLOps
Databricks (nice to have)
MLflow (nice to have)




Position based in Brussels

Open to permanent employment (CDI) or freelance contracts

3 days of remote work per week







Why Join This Opportunity?

You'll become part of a highly skilled technical team working on impactful projects within the renewable energy sector. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to the industrialization of Machine Learning solutions that support the energy transition while working with modern cloud and data technologies.
