Title: ML & GenAI Team Lead (Sagacify)
Description:
Craftzing and Sagacify are looking for an ML & GenAI Team Lead who is equally at home in a pull request as in a people conversation. Someone who does not see leadership and technical depth as a trade-off, but as two things that make each other better.

Do you get energy from building high-performing teams while staying close to the code and the architecture? Then you will probably feel right at home here.

In this role you help build and steer a team of ML and AI Engineers at Sagacify, part of Craftzing, while staying an active contributor on client projects that actually ship. You sit at the intersection of teams, offices and disciplines - reporting to the Head of ML at Sagacify and collaborating closely with the Craftzing delivery organisation. In practice, roughly 70% of your time goes to hands-on delivery and 30% to leading the team.

This is not a role where you hand the technical work off to your team. You stay in the code, you stay in the architecture — and you also grow the people around you.

What you'll do

Team leadership


Lead, mentor and grow a team of five ML and AI Engineers
Set and enforce quality standards, delivery frameworks and best practices
Foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation and knowledge sharing
Own key client relationships, ensuring satisfaction and long-term trust



AI Engineering


Architect end-to-end AI applications orchestrating LLMs, vector databases and APIs
Design and build autonomous agents and conversational systems
Engineer and optimise RAG pipelines connecting enterprise data to LLMs
Perform prompt and context engineering to improve output quality and reduce hallucinations
Evaluate system quality through generative AI metrics and production monitoring
Deploy and scale solutions with focus on latency, security and cost efficiency



MLOps


Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines and automated testing for model deployment
Monitor production models across drift, quality metrics and operational costs
Ensure versioning, reproducibility and safe rollout of models and AI services
Collaborate with DevOps and infrastructure teams for smooth delivery



What you'll work with

Core technologies


Python
OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers
LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse
FastAPI or Flask, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines
Cloud GPU
AWS (S3, SQS, IAM, RDS, etc.)
Cursor



Bonus points for experience with :


TypeScript
Azure
AI-native engineering workflows: hands-on experience using AI coding agents and AI-assisted development environments (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.), including context engineering, subagent orchestra…



You do not need to master every tool on this list. But you know the fundamentals cold, you pick things up fast, and you are honest about where your gaps are.

Who we're looking for


You have 5 to 9+ years of experience, including at least 2 to 3 years in a tech lead role in ML and AI projects
You have excellent communication skills and are comfortable presenting to C-level stakeholders and facilitating workshops
You have strong organisational skills and can manage multiple workstreams simultaneously
You know how to coach and grow talent while remaining individually impactful on delivery
You are curious and genuinely passionate about AI, with the ability to evaluate and adopt new tools quickly
You communicate fluently in Dutch and English; French is a strong plus



Don't worry if you don't tick every single box - what matters most is the right mindset and a drive to keep learning.

Why you'll love working here

Sagacify is part of Craftzing, and together we build AI solutions that create lasting impact for our clients. You will be part of a team of enthusiasts who love to learn, continuously develop new skills and fast-track our customers' AI journey across various industries.

At Craftzing and Sagacify, you get the trust and space to do your best work - in a human-sized environment with real team spirit, flexible working and room to take ownership.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor’s degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments.
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio.
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise.

Title: Data scientist _FR
Description:
Comment pouvez-vous faire la différence ?

En tant que Data scientist – dans le service Corporate strategy, vous développez et maintenez des modèles stratégiques qui fournissent des insights approfondis et qui contribuent directement aux choix politiques et aux décisions d’investissement.

Vous travaillez à l’intersection des données, du business et de la stratégie et vous veillez à ce que des hypothèses et calculs complexes soient implémentés de manière transparente, robuste et reproductible dans des solutions de données évolutives.

Quel est votre rôle à la SNCB ?

En tant que Data scientist, vous aurez les responsabilités suivantes :


Vous développez, maintenez et industrialisez des modèles stratégiques sur la base d’hypothèses métier, en collaboration avec les parties prenantes concernées.
Vous travaillez en étroite collaboration avec des collègues de différentes directions de la SNCB dans un contexte transversal à l’échelle de toute l’organisation.
Vous traduisez des modèles Excel existants, aujourd’hui gérés localement, en solutions PySpark robustes et évolutives.
Vous automatisez le traitement des données et l’exécution des modèles via des pipelines dans Databricks.
Vous travaillez avec Python et PySpark dans un environnement Azure Databricks pour traiter les données, implémenter des modèles et réaliser des analyses.
Vous veillez à la transparence, à la reproductibilité et au respect des bonnes pratiques lors de l’implémentation des modèles.
Vous explorez les opportunités d’intégrer des sources de données et des techniques analytiques supplémentaires (telles que les données spatiales, l’IA ou des visualisations avancées) lorsqu’elles apportent une réelle valeur ajoutée aux insights stratégiques.
Vous suivez activement les évolutions IA et machine learning et réfléchissez à la manière dont elles peuvent renforcer la modélisation stratégique.


Notre manière de travailler


Le développement se fait localement dans VS Code dans un repository Git partagé.
La collaboration se fait via des pull requests dans Azure DevOps.
Le déploiement et l’exécution se font dans Azure Databricks.
Nous travaillons selon une méthode agile, avec un développement itératif, des cycles de feedback courts et une collaboration étroite avec les parties prenantes.
Nous suivons les bonnes pratiques en matière de qualité de code, de gestion de versions et de collaboration.
Vous travaillez dans une équipe où le partage de connaissances, la transparence et la qualité sont essentiels.


Un job fait pour vous ?


Vous avez un master, de préférence en mathématiques, statistiques, data science, sciences de l’ingénieur ou une autre discipline quantitative (ou équivalent par expérience) ET une expérience de minimum 4 ans en tant que data scientist en industrie.


ET vous disposez des compétences suivantes :


Vous êtes doté.e d’une solide connaissance de Python pour le data wrangling, l’analyse de données et la modélisation.
Vous avez de l’expérience avec PySpark et le travail avec de grands volumes de données.
Vous avez de l’expérience dans l’industrialisation d’analyses ou de modèles (par exemple la migration d’Excel vers du code de production).
Vous communiquez aisément, aussi bien en français qu’en anglais et pouvez expliquer clairement des modèles complexes et des hypothèses à des parties prenantes non techniques.
Vous avez un esprit structuré et êtes orienté.e solution et qualité.
Vous avez un intérêt pour le machine learning, les applications d’IA, les données géographiques ou outils GIS (par ex. QGIS, ArcGIS), le transport, la mobilité ou des systèmes opérationnels complexes ? Il s’agit d’atouts en plus.


Si vous avez obtenu votre diplôme à l'étranger, vous devez être en possession d'une attestation d'équivalence délivrée par la Communauté française pour pouvoir participer à la sélection.

Qu'avons-nous à vous offrir ?

Choisir la SNCB, c'est choisir un environnement où vous pouvez apprendre et vous épanouir. Vous serez entouré de chouettes collègues qui vous soutiendront. En tant que Data Scientist, vous recevez un package salarial qui se compose de :


Un salaire attractif complété d'un 13e mois, d'une indemnité de frais, des chèques-repas et d'un bonus collectif et individuel.
Une partie du salaire est offerte sous forme de budget flexible. Vous pouvez opter pour la location d'un vélo, une voiture en leasing, pour le remboursement de l'épargne-pension, etc.
Une assurance hospitalisation et collective.

HTML:
Comment pouvez-vous faire la différence ?

En tant que Data scientist – dans le service Corporate strategy, vous développez et maintenez des modèles stratégiques qui fournissent des insights approfondis et qui contribuent directement aux choix politiques et aux décisions d’investissement.

Vous travaillez à l’intersection des données, du business et de la stratégie et vous veillez à ce que des hypothèses et calculs complexes soient implémentés de manière transparente, robuste et reproductible dans des solutions de données évolutives.

Quel est votre rôle à la SNCB ?

En tant que Data scientist, vous aurez les responsabilités suivantes :

  • Vous développez, maintenez et industrialisez des modèles stratégiques sur la base d’hypothèses métier, en collaboration avec les parties prenantes concernées.
  • Vous travaillez en étroite collaboration avec des collègues de différentes directions de la SNCB dans un contexte transversal à l’échelle de toute l’organisation.
  • Vous traduisez des modèles Excel existants, aujourd’hui gérés localement, en solutions PySpark robustes et évolutives.
  • Vous automatisez le traitement des données et l’exécution des modèles via des pipelines dans Databricks.
  • Vous travaillez avec Python et PySpark dans un environnement Azure Databricks pour traiter les données, implémenter des modèles et réaliser des analyses.
  • Vous veillez à la transparence, à la reproductibilité et au respect des bonnes pratiques lors de l’implémentation des modèles.
  • Vous explorez les opportunités d’intégrer des sources de données et des techniques analytiques supplémentaires (telles que les données spatiales, l’IA ou des visualisations avancées) lorsqu’elles apportent une réelle valeur ajoutée aux insights stratégiques.
  • Vous suivez activement les évolutions IA et machine learning et réfléchissez à la manière dont elles peuvent renforcer la modélisation stratégique.

Notre manière de travailler

  • Le développement se fait localement dans VS Code dans un repository Git partagé.
  • La collaboration se fait via des pull requests dans Azure DevOps.
  • Le déploiement et l’exécution se font dans Azure Databricks.
  • Nous travaillons selon une méthode agile, avec un développement itératif, des cycles de feedback courts et une collaboration étroite avec les parties prenantes.
  • Nous suivons les bonnes pratiques en matière de qualité de code, de gestion de versions et de collaboration.
  • Vous travaillez dans une équipe où le partage de connaissances, la transparence et la qualité sont essentiels.

Un job fait pour vous ?

  • Vous avez un master, de préférence en mathématiques, statistiques, data science, sciences de l’ingénieur ou une autre discipline quantitative (ou équivalent par expérience) ET une expérience de minimum 4 ans en tant que data scientist en industrie.

ET vous disposez des compétences suivantes :

  • Vous êtes doté.e d’une solide connaissance de Python pour le data wrangling, l’analyse de données et la modélisation.
  • Vous avez de l’expérience avec PySpark et le travail avec de grands volumes de données.
  • Vous avez de l’expérience dans l’industrialisation d’analyses ou de modèles (par exemple la migration d’Excel vers du code de production).

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
ML OPS Engineer / AI Engineer

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international group based in Denmark and Belgium, comprising over 350 consultants dedicated to unlocking the full business potential of companies through technology and digital transformation.

We have a specialized Financial Services practice that is experiencing rapid growth. Over 100 consultants focus exclusively on the financial services sector, assisting banks and insurances in redesigning their operations to meet changing client expectations, adopt new technologies, and adhere to evolving regulations (DORA, RIS, AI Act, AML, KYC, etc).

At NORRIQ, our employees are at the heart of our organization and our success.. We hire and nurture passionate individuals who strive for excellence for both our clients and colleagues. Our culture supports personal and professional growth with training, opportunities, and creative freedom. Regardless of when you join or how long you stay, you'll experience an unforgettable NORRIQ journey and become a part of our extended family. Connected forever!

Role Overview

As an MLOps Engineer / AI Engineer, you will play a central role in bridging Data Science and IT Operations. You will design, deploy, and scale production-grade machine learning and AI solutions for our financial services clients. Your mission is to ensure robust, secure, and efficient AI operations—covering deployment, observability, lifecycle management, and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for someone who loves building AI infrastructure, optimizing performance, and enabling data scientists to move faster and safer.

Key Responsibilities


Deploy ML/AI Models: Work with Data Scientists to deploy secure, scalable, and reliable models into production.
Build CI/CD Pipelines: Automate testing, packaging, and deployment for data and model workflows.
Manage Data Pipelines: Design and maintain efficient data flows across storage systems, databases, and platforms.
Set Up Environments: Manage dependencies, development environments, and tooling for smooth project onboarding.
Operate Kubernetes: Deploy and maintain containerized AI components using Kubernetes.
Monitor & Troubleshoot: Oversee model/application performance, resolve incidents, and conduct root-cause analyses.
Optimize & Innovate: Identify improvements, adopt new technologies, and enhance AI engineering practices.


Requirements

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong background in programming, system engineering, or DevOps is highly valued.


Technical Skills


Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience with REST APIs and Git.
Hands-on expertise in CI/CD and MLOps/DevOps practices.
Practical experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Familiarity with Linux, software testing, and modern software engineering workflows.
Nice to have: SQL/PostgreSQL, MLflow, Helm, or related tooling.


Soft Skills


Collaborative and team-oriented mindset.
Proactive, solution-driven, and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Strong communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.


Languages


Fluent in English (spoken and written).
Knowledge of additional languages (Dutch or French) is a plus.


Why Joining Us?


You will be part of a fast growing international company with a unique people culture
A very competitive salary package including a company car, iPhone and different allowances
You will get extensive trainings in Belgium and Denmark on technical matters, as well as soft skills and project management, and you will have access to new technologies and innovative equipment.
You will join a dynamic and growing team with a great mix of young and experienced professionals focusing on financial services.

Title: Consultant in Data Science
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



As a consultant in Data Science, you will be involved in transformation projects at our clients. You will execute project assignments in modelling and digital transformation (business/IT analysis and partial coding) environments with a good deal of business perspectives:



Machine learning, AI and statistics: developing predictive models of decision making (supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, econometrics, forecasting, quantitative diagnosis,...)
Operational research : assistance in the design and implementation of constraint optimization solutions and simulation models
Big Data : Algorithmic knowledge for large and unstructured data processing

You will get the opportunity to actively contribute to Sia's internal activities:



Development and reinforcement of our offer, by understanding and interpreting data science innovation and challenges
Business development, by contributing to need definition and participating in our commercial initiatives

Equipped with the entrepreneurial fibre, you are ready to cooperate in a fast growing company.



In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


University Master Civil Engineer (applied mathematics, IT or similar)
0 to 3 years’ experience as a consultant
Outstanding team player with great interpersonal and communication skills
Strong interest in data science and related sectors such as Energy and Finance
Analytical mind, exploring options and able to synthesize into conclusions
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Dynamic, result oriented, rigorous and committed
Trilingual: French, Dutch and English
Prepared to participate in temporary assignments abroad


Additional Information



We are unable to provide a work permit for this position



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Reward Consultant
Description:
Partena Professional, expert en ressources humaines et entrepreneuriat, est l'une des plus grandes entreprises belges organisées selon le modèle de gouvernance collaborative. Autonomie et responsabilité, agilité et innovation sont aujourd'hui nos maîtres-mots.

Pour renforcer notre équipe Total Reward, nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) Reward Consultant pour notre agence à Erembodegem.

Les plans cafétaria vous intéressent et vous avez toujours voulu en savoir plus ? La législation sociale et la paie n’ont que peu de secrets pour vous ? Vous aimez travailler de manière autonome tout en profitant d’un excellent esprit d’équipe ?

Vous pouvez exceller dans tout cela dans le poste de Reward Consultant !

💼 Qu’attendons-nous de vous ?


Vous pilotez l’implémentation complète du plan cafétéria : workshops, configuration et tests de l’outil, ainsi que le chargement des données.
Vous organisez des sessions d’information pour les employés et répondez aux questions des clients sur le plan cafétéria.
Vous accompagnez les clients lors de la réouverture de leur plan et assurez un suivi fluide.
Vous gérez votre travail de manière autonome, tout en respectant les délais fixés.
Vous maintenez vos connaissances à jour et développez continuellement vos compétences.



Ce que nous offrons :

🏡 Flexibilité et équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée :


Possibilité de télétravail et horaires flexibles
20 jours de congés légaux + 12 jours de congés extra-légaux (contrat de 39h/semaine)
3 jours de congé extralégaux supplémentaires
1 jour de congé supplémentaire tous les 5 ans d’ancienneté



💶 Un package salarial attractif et compétitif :


Contrat à durée indéterminée, prime de fin d’année et double pécule de vacances
Indemnité nette mensuelle
Chèques repas de 10€/jour
Écochèques jusqu’à 250 €/an
Laptop et GSM
Voiture de société avec carte de recharge
Assurance groupe et assurance hospitalisation
Plan cafétéria



🎓 Des possibilités de développement :

Vous aurez des opportunités d’évolution au sein de votre propre équipe, mais aussi dans notre groupe qui compte environ 1 800 collaborateurs.

Vous pouvez également exercer des rôles complémentaires en plus de votre fonction principale.

Grâce à notre Académie interne, vous avez accès à des formations et ateliers pour élargir vos connaissances et renforcer vos compétences.

La force de nos équipes réside dans les qualités de chacun de nos collaborateurs.

La diversité de nos équipes est ce qui rend nos services si puissants.

Vous avez un diplôme de bachelier ou équivalent par expérience, et vous avez déjà une expérience en payroll et en législation sociale
Vous avez un niveau C1 en néerlandais, la connaissance du français et de l'anglais sont des atouts.

Title: Crew Member - Student Job - Brussels
Description:
Since 1986, we've done things the same way: fresh ingredients, burgers made your way, and fries made to perfection.

We started as a family business and, honestly, we still feel like one - just with more locations, more teammates, and a lot more burgers. We look out for each other, work hard, have fun, and get the job done.

If that sounds like your kind of place, you might be our next Crew Member.

What You'll Do

As a Crew Member, you’re a big part of the Five Guys experience. No two shifts are the same. You’ll:


Help customers choose their perfect burger 🍔
Cook and assemble fresh burgers and fries
Take orders, welcome guests, and keep the restaurant running smoothly
Deliver great service with a smile (we’re just as proud of our service as our food)


You’ll learn every part of the restaurant, and we’ll support you every step of the way. Think: earn while you learn.

Grow, Compete & Earn More


Secret Shopper Bonus Program – get rewarded for great service and quality
Five Guys Games – every two years, crew members worldwide compete for cash prizes and the title of Ultimate Grill Master 🔥


What We're Looking For

You don’t need experience - just the right mindset.


You’re 16 years or older
You bring energy and enjoy working as part of a team
You can handle a fast-paced environment

Title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
Description:
Service description:




We are looking for a Software Engineer Service to join one of our software engineering teams focused on developing AI-centric applications.

The role combines strong software engineering skills with practical experience in building solutions using LLMs, RAG, AI assistants, and agentic workflows. The ideal candidate can help design, implement, evaluate, and improve AI-powered applications that are reliable, secure, maintainable, and useful in a real business environment.

This is a hands-on engineering role. We are looking for someone who can build production-quality solutions, contribute to technical direction, and support the team in applying AI engineering practices effectively.




Tasks:







Design and implement AI-powered applications using LLMs, RAG, and agentic workflow patterns.
Build backend services, APIs, and integrations needed for AI-centric applications.
Design and improve retrieval pipelines, including chunking, embeddings, vector search, hybrid search, metadata filtering, and reranking.
Work with LLM APIs and AI orchestration frameworks to build reliable workflows.
Implement evaluation, testing, monitoring, and observability for AI applications.
Help define safe and practical patterns for tool use, human-in-the-loop approval, and agentic behavior.
Collaborate with other engineers and product/business stakeholders.
Support model and framework selection based on quality, cost, latency, maintainability, and security.
Help troubleshoot issues such as hallucinations, poor retrieval quality, high latency, cost inefficiency, and unreliable outputs.




Requirements:

You are able to speak, read and write fluently English and French or Dutch.
Minimum 5 years of experience as Software Engineer, preferably in backend or full-stack development.
Minimum 1 to 2 years of experience integrating LLMs or other generative AI services into software applications.
Practical knowledge of RAG, embeddings, vector search, and retrieval quality improvement.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Experience with MCP, A2A, tool calling, or multi-agent workflows.
Experience designing maintainable services with proper testing, logging, CI/CD, and deployment practices.
Understanding of AI application evaluation, including test datasets, quality metrics, regression testing, and user feedback.
Good understanding of cloud-native application development.
Security-conscious mindset when working with internal or sensitive data.
Ability to communicate technical trade-offs clearly and pragmatically.
Ability to distinguish when AI, RAG, or agents are useful and when a simpler deterministic solution is better.
Nice to Have
Experience with AI frameworks such as LangGraph, LangChain, Semantic Kernel.
Experience with observability or evaluation tools.
Familiarity with Azure cloud environment.
Experience with Microsoft AI ecosystem tools such as Semantic Kernel, Microsoft
Agent Framework, Microsoft Foundry, or Microsoft 365 Agents SDK.
Experience with .NET / C#.
Experience with vector databases or enterprise search platforms.
Experience working in regulated, security-sensitive, or enterprise environments.
The right consultant should be comfortable working across software engineering, AI integration, evaluation, security, and production readiness. They should be able to move fast when building MVPs, while still applying enough engineering discipline to make successful solutions scalable and maintainable.




Additional information:

Location: Brussels

Title: Data Scientist - GenAI
Description:
Join Artefact Belgium: Shape the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Are you an early-career professional excited to harness the power of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is seeking a Data Scientist - Gen AI to join our innovative team in Brussels. Our Belgian team specializes in LLM and GenAI projects, and this is your chance to work on cutting-edge solutions, collaborate with top-tier experts, and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic, forward-thinking environment.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Motivated to apply your technical expertise to real-world challenges in AI. Able to thrive in collaborative settings while independently driving your own workstreams. Eager to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible in AI innovation.

Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. At Artefact, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, with a strong commitment to inclusivity and employee growth.

What You Will Do


Collaborate with the team to design, train, and fine-tune state-of-the-art models for client projects and internal tools.
Develop and integrate agentic AI solutions using tool calling, and emerging protocols like Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create autonomous, context-aware applications.
Research and implement innovative generative AI applications to solve complex business problems.
Design and implement comprehensive evaluation frameworks to measure LLM performance, establishing quality metrics and benchmarks that guide development decisions and ensure reliable, production-ready solutions.
Optimize the performance, scalability, and usability of LLM-powered products and tools.
Contribute to the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including models, APIs, and supporting infrastructure.
Participate in technical discussions and brainstorming sessions to shape impactful solutions.


What You Bring


A degree in computer science, mathematics, engineering, physics, or a related field.
Experience working with LLMs, generative AI, or advanced NLP applications (e.g., fine-tuning models, using frameworks like Hugging Face or OpenAI).
Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with AI and NLP libraries.
Analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with an ability to address real-world challenges through AI.
Good communication skills in English, Dutch or French; additional languages are a plus.
A growth mindset, adaptability, and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment.


What We Offer


A competitive salary with benefits tailored for early-career professionals.
Hands-on experience with state-of-the-art LLM technologies in impactful projects.
A dynamic and supportive work environment that fosters creativity and professional development.
Resources for ongoing training, certification, and skill-building in generative AI and related fields.
A collaborative, inclusive workplace with access to global expertise through Artefact's network.


This position is based in Brussels.

Don't worry if you don't meet every criterion! We value passion, potential, and a commitment to innovation. If you're excited about generative AI and ready to grow your career, we'd love to hear from you!

Title: Researcher, China
Description:
FULL-TIME JOB VACANCY

Researcher, China

Asia Division

Multiple Locations Considered

Application deadline: 14 July, 2026




The Asia Division of Human Rights Watch (HRW) is seeking a Researcher who will investigate, analyze, and advocate against human rights abuses in China. The Asia Division is one of HRW’s six regional research programs and is made up of about 30 staff, many of them based in the region and working in challenging environments.

This position reports to the Deputy Asia Director. The full-time position will preferably be based within commuting distance of one of HRW’s offices, though locations in the Asia region may be considered.




Responsibilities:

Research, monitor and document the human rights situation in China by collecting and analyzing information from a wide variety of sources including governments, media, academia, NGOs, victims, witnesses, journalists, diplomats, security forces and others;
Write and publicize reports, briefing papers, letters, news releases, op-eds, and submissions to international bodies; collect supporting documents and materials such as photographs and videos;
Assist in the development and implementation of local, regional, and international strategies to change abusive laws, policies, and practices;
Respond to queries from the media, public, and colleagues in the human rights community and take initiative to project concerns in public debates;
Assist the Director of the Division and HRW’s Development and Global Initiative Department in conducting fundraising, when needed;
Place abuses within the broader political, social and economic contexts and present human rights concerns to government officials, opinion leaders, inter-governmental agencies, and the media;
Work closely with non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and local human rights organizations to ensure that HRW's work complements and enhances their own work without jeopardizing their security;
Liaise effectively with HRW staff located in multiple locations throughout the world to ensure effective coordination and delivery of activities;
Travel internationally, as required;
Deliver outputs in a timely manner and consistent with the agreed strategy and priorities of the Asia division, and
Perform other tasks, as may be required.




Qualifications:

Education: An advanced (graduate) degree or equivalent experience in international relations, journalism, law, social sciences, or related studies is required.

Experience: A minimum of five years of professional-level experience working on China is required. Experience working in or knowledge of China’s civil society, media, social media and government policies is preferred.

Related Skills and Knowledge:

Ability to identify, research, analyze and effectively communicate important human rights developments in China is required.
Ability to conduct interviews safely remotely and in-person with a wide range of people, including victims and witnesses, is required. Ability to conduct research through open-sourced investigation techniques is preferred.
Knowledge of and experience working in international human rights and familiarity with international human rights law are required.
Excellent oral and written communication skills in English and Mandarin are required.
Ability to research abuses related to technology and/or those in the Tibetan areas is preferred.
Initiative and follow-through, capacity to think creatively and strategically, excellent editing skills, and dynamic public speaking skills are required.
Interpersonal skills in order to work collaboratively within HRW, as well as with external partners, are required.
Ability to multi-task effectively, including having strong planning and organizing skills, and ability to work under pressure are required.
Ability to work flexible hours to accommodate developing and urgent events is required.




Salary and Benefits: HRW seeks exceptional applicants and offers comprehensive compensation and benefits. HRW can offer a relocation assistance package and immigration support for this role if required, and people of all nationalities are encouraged to apply.




How to Apply: Please apply by 14 July 2026, by visiting our online job portal at careers.hrw.org and submitting a cover letter and CV or resume. No calls or email inquiries, please. Only complete applications will be reviewed and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

If you are experiencing technical difficulties with your application submission, or if you require a disability-related accommodation, please email recruitment@hrw.org. Due to the large response, application submissions via email will not be accepted and inquiries regarding the status of applications will go unanswered.




Human Rights Watch is strong because it is diverse. We actively seek a diverse applicant pool and encourage candidates of all backgrounds to apply. Human Rights Watch does not discriminate on the basis of disability, age, gender identity and expression, national origin, race and ethnicity, religious beliefs, sexual orientation, or criminal record. We welcome all kinds of diversity. Our employees include people who are parents and nonparents, the self-taught and university educated, and from a wide span of socio-economic backgrounds and perspectives on the world. Human Rights Watch is an equal opportunity employer.




Human Rights Watch is an international human rights monitoring and advocacy organization known for its in-depth investigations, its incisive and timely reporting, its innovative and high-profile advocacy campaigns, and its success in changing the human rights-related policies and practices of influential governments and international institutions.

Title: Business Development Representative
Description:
Company Description

Bleen is a Brussels-based boutique consultancy specialized in sustainability. Our mission is to help organizations transition toward decarbonized, regenerative, and distributive models. We are committed to building a more sustainable economy and driving meaningful environmental and social impact. Join us in reimagining our economy and in making a real difference.







Role Description

This is a full-time, on-site Business Development Representative role based in Brussels.

The Business Development Representative will identify and qualify new business opportunities through research, outreach, and lead generation activities across targeted markets and sectors. On a day-to-day basis, this role will engage prospective clients to understand their sustainability needs, present Bleen’s consulting services, and nurture relationships through the sales pipeline. The role will support the development of proposals, prepare sales materials, and maintain accurate records in the CRM.




Qualifications

Strong sales and business development capabilities, with 0 to 2 years of experience.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with professional fluency in French, Dutch, and English.
Strong interest in sustainability and motivation to support systemic change within organizations.
Comfort working in a fast-paced boutique environment: small team, big scope.
Master’s degree in Business Administration or a related field.
Optimistic by nature, with the conviction that we can drive meaningful change and help shape a better future.

Title: Data Engineer (Databricks) - Freelance - Long term project - Brussels - Competitive market rate
Description:
Data Engineer (Databricks) – Freelance Contract

Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)

Long-Term Freelance Contract

Competitive Market Rate




We are currently looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong Databricks expertise to join a long-term transformation programme for a leading organisation based in Brussels.

This is an excellent opportunity to work on large-scale data initiatives, helping to build and optimise modern data platforms in a cloud-based environment.




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks.
Build and optimise ETL/ELT processes for large and complex datasets.
Work closely with Data Architects, Data Analysts, and business stakeholders to deliver data solutions.
Develop and maintain data models to support reporting, analytics, and AI initiatives.
Ensure data quality, reliability, and performance across the data platform.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of data engineering best practices and standards.




Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Data Engineer in enterprise environments.
Strong hands-on experience with Databricks.
Excellent knowledge of SQL and Python.
Experience building data pipelines and data integration solutions.
Knowledge of cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS, or GCP.
Experience working with large-scale data lakes and modern data architectures.
Strong communication skills and ability to work within cross-functional teams.




Nice to Have

Experience with Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, or Microsoft Fabric.
Knowledge of CI/CD and DevOps practices.

Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)

Long-Term Freelance Contract

Competitive Market Rate


We are currently looking for an experienced Data Engineer with strong Databricks expertise to join a long-term transformation programme for a leading organisation based in Brussels.

This is an excellent opportunity to work on large-scale data initiatives, helping to build and optimise modern data platforms in a cloud-based environment.


Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks.
  • Build and optimise ETL/ELT processes for large and complex datasets.
  • Work closely with Data Architects, Data Analysts, and business stakeholders to deliver data solutions.

Required Skills & Experience

Nice to Have

Title: Genetic Counselor
Description:
UZ Brussel: Samen sterk in zorg, innovatie en wetenschap

Word onze Genetic Counselor Medische Genetica

UZ Brussel is een toonaangevend universitair ziekenhuis in het hart van onze hoofdstad. Wij zijn een warm en vooruitstrevend ziekenhuis waar innovatie, onderzoek en patiëntgerichte zorg hand in hand gaan. Elke dag zetten bijna 5.000 medewerkers zich in om onze missie waar te maken: zorg bieden die niet alleen uitblinkt in kwaliteit, maar ook in menselijkheid.

Wil jij met jouw talent het verschil maken voor onze patiënten? Dan ben jij de collega die we zoeken! We zoeken een gedreven consulent Ondersteuning Medische Genetica die samen met ons de zorg naar een hoger niveau tilt. Maak deel uit van ons team en bouw mee aan zorg die inspireert!

Hoe kan jij het verschil maken?

Wat ga je doen als Genetic Counselor:


Je werkt op het Centrum voor Medische Genetica van het UZ Brussel.
Je staat in voor het genetisch advies van patiënten met erfelijke aandoeningen.
Je informeert patiënten en hun familieleden over de aandoening, met inbegrip van de diagnose, de prognose en de verschillende mogelijkheden van preventie of behandeling.
Je biedt psychologische ondersteuning om de informatie te verwerken en te aanvaarden en coördineert aanvragen voor diagnostisch, predictief en dragerschapsonderzoek.
Je ondersteunt de klinisch geneticus en werkt in een multidisciplinair team.
Je neemt deel aan de klinische stafvergaderingen van het centrum.
Je helpt mee aan het opstellen van een (inter)nationaal register van patiënten met erfelijke aandoeningen.
Je ondersteunt het opstellen, coördineren en uitvoeren van klinische studies bij patiënten met erfelijke aandoeningen.
Je draagt bij aan het uitwerken en opstellen van klinische richtlijnen.
Je verleent poliklinische en administratieve ondersteuning bij de follow-up van jonge kinderen geboren na geassisteerde reproductieve technieken (ART).
Je hebt een coördinerende functie uit te oefenen tussen patiënt, administratie en artsen om het zorg traject voor de patiënt optimaal te laten verlopen.



Welke collega zoeken we?

We zijn op zoek naar een empathische genetic counselor die zich herkent in de volgende eigenschappen:


Empathisch en geïnteresseerd in contact met patiënten: Je speelt in op de (zorg)behoeften en noden van patiënten en hun familie. Je hebt oog voor je eigen welzijn en dat van collega’s.
Een health care professional-achtergrond (bv. in het bezit van een diploma verpleegkunde, vroedkunde, ..).
Kennis van erfelijke aandoeningen: Bij voorkeur heb je een bijkomende opleiding genetic counseling gevolgd of je bent bereid deze te volgen.
Taalvaardig: Je communiceert helder en gestructureerd. Je hebt een zeer goede beheersing van het Nederlands, Frans (zowel mondeling als schriftelijk) en een goede beheersing van het Engels.
Initiatiefnemer: Je ziet kansen en je onderneemt actie.
Organisatorisch sterk: Je bepaalt effectief doelen en prioriteiten, plant de nodige acties en gebruikt tijd en middelen efficiënt.
Teamspeler: Je werkt graag in een multidisciplinaire setting. Je kan zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband werken. Je draagt bij aan een positieve werksfeer waar voortdurend leren, ontwikkelen en samenwerken op de voorgrond staat.
Ervaring met databanken is een pluspunt.



Daarnaast erken je de kernwaarden van UZ Brussel: de patiënt komt altijd op de eerste plaats, met respect voor hem of haar als mens, ongeacht zijn afkomst, taal, sociale status of filosofische overtuiging, en met respect voor zijn recht op zelfbeschikking. Het UZ Brussel is meer dan een ziekenhuis, het staat voor een verdraagzame samenleving waar wederzijds respect de essentie is. Een humanistische houding wordt gecombineerd met medische expertise die vertrekt van wetenschappelijk onderzoek en doorlopende innovatie.

Wat kunnen wij jou bieden?

Bij UZ Brussel bieden we meer dan een job. We bieden een omgeving waarin je écht een verschil kunt maken – voor patiënten én voor jezelf.


Contract op maat: Een contract (100%) van onbepaalde duur.
Waardering en groei: Bij UZ Brussel erkennen we jouw inzet en stimuleren we jouw talenten. We investeren in jouw persoonlijke en professionele ontwikkeling via een uitgebreid opleidingsaanbod en doorgroeimogelijkheden.
Een warm team: Je wordt omringd door enthousiaste en ervaren collega’s die je met open armen ontvangen.
Competitieve verloning volgens IFIC: Inclusief overname van relevante anciënniteit.
Extralegale voordelen:
Hospitalisatieverzekering, inclusief een tegemoetkoming in ambulante zorgen binnen het UZ Brussel
Aanvullend pensioenfonds en verzekering gewaarborgd inkomen.
Vergoeding auto/motor, volledige terugbetaling voor NMBS, De lijn en MIVB, fietsvergoeding of mogelijkheid tot fietsleasing.
Korting in ons bedrijfsrestaurant en toegang tot personeelsvoordelen via Benefits at Work.
Eindejaarspremie
27 vakantiedagen (20 wettelijke, 4 CAO en 3 UZB dagen), aangevuld met extra verlofdagen per 5 jaar anciënniteit (max. 3), CAO 50 + dagen
Korting voor jaarabonnement bij Basic Fit om jouw fysieke en mentale gezondheid te ondersteunen.
Jaarlijks gratis vaccinaties tegen de griep en, indien nodig, COVID-vaccinaties of boosters.
Mogelijkheid tot vakantieopvang voor kinderen (2,5-12 jaar) en kinderkribbe volgens beschikbaarheid op de VUB Campus.

Bij UZ Brussel geloven we in een gezonde balans tussen werk en privé, met aandacht voor jouw welzijn en persoonlijke groei.

Interesse?

Ben jij die empatische genetic counselor die een verschil wil maken op de dienst medische genetica? Aarzel niet langer en solliciteer nu via de onderstaande link voor deze uitdagende job.

Title: Data Engineering Internship
HTML:
Job Location

Brussels

Job Description

Are you passionate about technology and eager to kick-start your career in data engineering?

Join P&G as a Data Engineering Intern and be part of a dynamic team that harnesses groundbreaking technology to transform data into actionable insights. You will have the opportunity to work on innovative projects that directly impact our operations and contribute to the efficiency, resilience, and sustainability of one of the largest consumer goods companies in the world.

You will work closely with our data engineering, analytics, and supply chain teams to develop robust data pipelines and support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

Find out more about IT at P&G at www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ial3aeA9SE.

YOUR CONTRIBUTION TO P&G’S SUCCESS:

  • Data Pipeline Development: Assist in designing, implementing, and maintaining data pipelines to ensure seamless integration of data from various sources.
  • Data Management: Support data quality initiatives by monitoring and improving data integrity and availability across systems.
  • Collaboration: Work with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that meet business needs.
  • Data Analytics: Use data analytics to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for process improvements within supply chain and manufacturing.
  • Tool Development: Contribute to the development of data tools and dashboards that visualize insights and enhance decision-making.
  • Learning & Growth: Participate in training sessions and mentorship programs to enhance your technical skills and understanding of data engineering best practices.

We are offering internships of at least 6 months throughout the year, as of September 2026.

If studying at a Belgian university, please make sure you consult with the internship department at your school to agree on the paid internship we offer.

Job Qualifications

YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF YOU:

  • Are currently pursuing a Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, Engineering, Data Science, or another related quantitative field;
  • Have familiarity with programming languages such as Python, SQL, R, or similar;
  • Have basic knowledge of data management, data pipelines, databases, and data analytics concepts;
  • Have strong analytical skills and a passion for problem-solving;
  • Are adaptable and able to handle multiple priorities effectively, both independently and as part of a collaborative team;
  • Have effective communication skills and the ability to collaborate with diverse teams;
  • Are proactive and eager to learn new technologies, tools, and methodologies;
  • Are proficient in English, both written and spoken.

YOUR ADVANTAGES:

  • A temporary contract for the duration of your internship, with an attractive compensation package;
  • Monthly allowance: 2414 EUR gross/month, prorated 13th month based on the months worked during your internship period;
  • A flexible benefits plan to customize to your personal needs and kilometer allowance/public transport reduction;
  • Access to Benefits at Work platform: a large benefits platform with employee discounts on renowned shops and online commercial platforms, as well as on leisure activities and well-known amusement parks;
  • Working in an international and inclusive environment, with diverse and supportive colleagues;
  • Continuous self-development via coaching, trainings, and a general Growth Mindset working environment;
  • A vibrant and modern working environment supporting employees’ wellbeing:
    • Subsidized company restaurant;
    • On-site vibrant café for breaks and less formal meetings;
    • On-site fitness room and possibility to join regular group sport activities;
    • Flexibility in terms of working hours;
    • Employee Assistance Program: free professional counseling services, work-life resources, and mental health support.
    • Supporting employees’ commute: plenty of parking facilities for both cars and bikes, shower facilities, shuttle between Heizel metro and the office, and carpooling community.
ARE YOU EXCITED ABOUT THE ROLE? DEEP DIVE INTO P&G AND CHECK THE BELOW INSIGHTFUL LINKS:

Our hiring process consists of three main steps: 1) application, 2) online assessments and 3) interviews. Click here to get all the tips to be fully prepared.

P&G is driven to make life better, not just within the company, but across the globe. Click here to check out how we make a meaningful impact on the world.

We are the world’s largest consumer goods company. Click here to discover more about our iconic brands.

At P&G #weseeequal

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G, we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process. Please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment. We thank you in advance for your patience.

Full time

R000153931

Internships

Starting Pay / Salary Range

€2.414,00 / month

Title: Data Engineer - QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

As a Data Engineer I, you will build the foundational data infrastructure that powers cutting-edge AI applications leveraging LLMs, retrieval systems, workflows, and emerging agentic architectures.

You will design and maintain scalable data pipelines, manage secure data environments, and prepare data for AI-driven systems while collaborating with cross-functional teams and clients. You’ll tackle real-world challenges by contributing to the development of next-generation AI systems and grow as a technologist by working alongside diverse experts across industries.

In this role, you will design and build the scalable, reproducible data components essential for machine learning, agentic, and autonomous AI systems. You’ll assess data landscapes, apply data quality fundamentals, and prepare data for AI solutions. Additionally, you’ll learn to translate simple hypotheses into engineered features, manage secure data environments, and contribute to R&D initiatives focused on innovating and scaling next-generation AI capabilities.

Your work will help solve some of the most complex and high-impact challenges facing clients across industries. Collaborating across McKinsey’s QuantumBlack and Labs teams, you’ll help develop innovative AI capabilities and scalable enterprise solutions. You’ll help build robust data foundations for scalable, production-ready AI systems. Your contributions will directly support the firm’s ability to accelerate AI adoption, solve complex business problems at scale, and enable clients to achieve meaningful, lasting impact.

You’ll be based in one of our BeNeLux offices as part of our global Data Engineering community. Working in cross-functional Agile teams, you’ll collaborate closely with Data Scientists, Machine Learning Engineers, and industry experts to deliver AI solutions. By partnering with clients from data owners to C-level executives you’ll help solve complex problems that drive tangible business value and begin to build your skills as a client-facing technologist.

This role offers a unique opportunity to grow at the forefront of AI, data engineering, and emerging agentic technologies. You’ll develop expertise at the intersection of technology and business by tackling diverse challenges in the evolving AI landscape. Working alongside multidisciplinary teams, you’ll gain a holistic understanding of how data engineering enables advanced AI while collaborating with some of the leading AI and data experts in the industry.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Degree in Computer Science/Engineering, or equivalent experience.
0-2+ years’ experience in a data-focused role (internships, academic projects).
Ability to write clean, well-documented code in a modern programming language, with a strong preference for Python and SQL.
Interest in or exposure to data engineering in the context of Agentic AI, Generative AI, Machine Learning, or Business Intelligence across different types of data formats (structured vs unstructured) and processing methods (streaming vs batch).
Familiarity with commonly used data platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, PSQL, etc.), cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and engineering tools (Pandas, Spark, dbt, LangChain, etc.).
Exposure to modern software development practices, including version control (Git) and foundational concepts in DevOps and MLOps/LLMOps and CI/CD principles.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, in English.
Exceptional time management in a complex and largely autonomous work environment.
Willingness to learn quickly and adapt to different project situations and tech stacks.
Experience using coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, etc.) is a plus.

Title: Innovation Consultant
Description:
✓ Intrigued by innovation and R&D?




✓ Entrepreneurial and thriving in a global environment 🌐`

✓ Ready to become fully responsible for your own projects & clients




For an established business performance consultancy with HQ at the edge of Brussels we are currently looking for an innovation consultantto join their tribe!




They are currently active in over 15 countries and manage more than 15.000 projects in R&D and innovation on a yearly basis.




Their innovation performance team consists out of 6 colleagues with backgrounds in science, economics and law who are working - in-house - on several innovation projects with enterprise level clients.




THE ROLE

As a trusted advisor for your clients you will work on cases around R&D strategy, tax & grants optimization and subsidy opportunities




You will manage your own projects from intake to presentation.
During the intake session with your clients you will asses their current situation and the challenges ahead.
After receiving all the necessary documents you will dive into the numbers and perform a fiscal analysisand get in touch with stakeholders (BELSPO and tax authorities)
After presenting your outcome to the clients you will also be their main point of contact during the implementation phase.




As you might have suspected - ownership is key in this position and you will stay on as account manager in charge.




YOUR PROFILE

Your friends perceive you as a challenger who has the creativity to reinvent, optimize and test the limits of the status quo.
Your MO always includes a thorough approach with attention to details
Between 2 and 10 years of experience in a similar role in the consulting field
A master's degree
You are fluent in Dutch & English




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT




You get the chance to join an international group with SME feel (office in Belgium with 50 colleagues)

Innovation and optimization are at the heart of what they do - providing you with an interesting career and learning curve

You will receive a competitive salary package, company car, attractive bonus system, insurances, mealvouchers, eco cheques and so on.




_ _ _

Sounds like you?

Apply today or reach out to hello@huntr.be for some extra info.

Title: ML & GenAI Team Lead
Description:
Want to create a positive impact through AI?

Founded as one of the first AI companies in Belgium with a mission to create a meaningful impact, we are believers in automation and efficiency. We see Artificial Intelligence as a toolbox that will allow us to automate more of the growing list of tasks that no longer require human-level cognition.

We are a tech team specialising in AI engineering, LLM systems, and MLOps. At Sagacify, part of Craftzing, you will find a collaborative human-sized environment with a great team spirit where you can bring ideas and take responsibilities in the areas you want to evolve in, regardless of your level of experience. We strive to create innovative digital solutions with a lasting impact, in a fun atmosphere where remote work, flexible schedules and regular team events are part of our culture.

We are looking for skilled people and team players to join the adventure.

Your Role

We're on the lookout for a Mid-Level / Senior ML & GenAI Team Lead who is equally at home in pull requests as in people conversations. Someone who does not see leadership and technical depth as a trade-off, but as two things that make each other better. In this role, you will help build and steer a high-performing team of ML and AI Engineers, while staying an active contributor on client projects that actually ship. In practice your time is split roughly between 30% of leading the team and 70% of hands-on delivery.

In this role you sit at the intersection of working across teams, offices, and disciplines. You report to the Head of ML at Sagacify and collaborate closely with the Craftzing delivery organisation.

What You'll Do

Team leadership


Lead, mentor and grow a team of five ML and AI Engineers
Set and enforce quality standards, delivery frameworks and best practices
Foster a culture of continuous learning, innovation and knowledge sharing
Own key client relationships, ensuring satisfaction and long-term trust


AI Engineering


Architect end-to-end AI applications orchestrating LLMs, vector databases and APIs
Design and build autonomous agents and conversational systems
Engineer and optimise RAG pipelines connecting enterprise data to LLMs
Perform prompt and context engineering to improve output quality and reduce hallucinations
Evaluate system quality through generative AI metrics and production monitoring
Deploy and scale solutions with focus on latency, security and cost efficiency


MLOps


Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines and automated testing for model deployment
Monitor production models across drift, quality metrics and operational costs
Ensure versioning, reproducibility and safe rollout of models and AI services
Collaborate with DevOps and infrastructure teams for smooth deliveryYou stay in the code, you stay in the architecture and you also grow the people around you.


What You’ll Work With

Core technologies :


Python
OpenAI API, Hugging Face Transformers
LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse
FastAPI or Flask, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines
Cloud GPU
AWS (S3, SQS, IAM, RDS, etc.)
Cursor


Bonus Points For Experience With


TypeScript
Azure
AI-native engineering workflows: hands-on experience using AI coding agents and AI-assisted development environments (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.), including context engineering, subagent orchestra…


About You


You have 5 to 9+ years of experience, including a minimum 2-3 years in a tech lead role in ML/AI projects
You have excellent communication skills, you're able to present to C-level stakeholders and facilitating workshops comfortably
You have strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple work-streams simultaneously
You have the ability to coach and grow talent while remaining individually impactful on delivery
You're curious and have a genuine passion for AI, with the ability to evaluate and adopt new tools quickly
You can communicate fluently in Dutch, English and French, or at least the first two languages


Don’t worry if you don’t tick every single box, what matters most is the right mindset and a drive to learn. If you think we’re a match, we’d love to hear from you.

Why You Will Love Working With Us

You'll be part of a team of enthusiasts who love to learn and continuously develop skills in different areas and technologies. You'll work with both the Sagacify and Craftzing teams, purposefully driven to create solutions that make a lasting difference, in an environment tailored to your needs.

Join us and contribute to the world of tomorrow by fast-tracking our customer’s AI journey in various industries.


Snacks, fruits and unlimited coffee
Net allowances, hospitalisation and group insurance
Hybrid and flexible working culture
Car & fuel or mobility budget
Pleasant working environment
Teambuildings and yearly holiday retreat


Recruiting process


Interview with the Head of Talent & Culture and the Tech Lead
Technical test
Meeting with the team (to discover the reality of the position)
Final interview & Offer
Show more

Title: Applications Scientist - Bioinformatics
Description:
Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?

At Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT), one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.

You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.

Our team at Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT), is united by a commitment to collaboration and scientific excellence, building on a strong foundation of innovation, expertise, and reliability. Guided by our vision to help researchers rapidly move from the lab to life-changing advances, we work closely with global partners to accelerate progress and genomics breakthroughs across fields like cancer, infectious disease, rare genetic disorders, and more. At IDT, you’ll be part of a culture rooted in continuous learning and improvement—where your growth fuels our mission to accelerate the pace of genomics and helps shape a healthier, brighter future for all.

Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.

The Applications Scientist – Bioinformatics role is responsible for aiding customers’ next generation sequencing (NGS) analyses and delivering pre‑ and post‑sales technical bioinformatic support for IDT’s NGS products.

This position reports to the Manager, Scientific Applications Support - NGS and is part of Scientific Applications Support department and will be fully remote.

In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To


Communicate with and support customers to provide pre- and post-sales technical support for a growing genomics-focused product offering and associated bioinformatics analysis platform. Maintain this knowledge in IDT’s growing database of technical information.
Answer or triage customer inquiries or software troubleshooting requests received by email promptly. Document all incoming and outgoing customer interactions in IDT’s Customer Relationship Management (CRM) database.
Support the complaint triage and investigation process and assist with complaint resolution by determining the cause of the problem, selecting and explaining the best solution to solve the problem, and expediting resolution to the customer’s satisfaction.
Provide technical expertise for customers (internal and external) regarding IDT’s NGS products, workflows, applications, and analysis. Escalate product questions, customer issues, or technical inquiries as needed.
Be able to communicate complex concepts and engage in highly technical conversations while building and maintaining relationships with customers.


The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, or related field. PhD is a plus.
At least 3 years of experience in processing and analyzing NGS datasets
Previous experience working in a customer-facing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Fluency in French or German (written and spoken) is a plus.


It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:


Basic script writing experience (Python preferred)
Hands-on experience with NGS library preparations and sequencing analysis
Familiarity with Archer and/or IDT NGS product offerings


At IDT, we believe in designing a better, more sustainable workforce. We recognize the benefits of flexible, remote working arrangements for eligible roles and are committed to providing enriching careers, no matter the work arrangement. This position is eligible for a remote work arrangement in which you can work remotely from your home. Additional information about this remote work arrangement will be provided by your interview team. Explore the flexibility and challenge that working for IDT can provide.

Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.

For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.

For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

Title: Data Scientist (m/w/d)
Description:
Wie macht man E-Commerce Logistik wirklich skalierbar? Unser Rocken Partner ist spezialisiert auf Fulfillment-Lösungen und Cross-border Versand für Online-Shops. Mit digitalisierten Prozessen – von Lagerung über Pick & Pack bis zur Retourenabwicklung – unterstützt das Unternehmen Händler dabei, international zu wachsen. Der Fokus liegt auf automatisierten Abläufen, massgeschneiderten Integrationen und der Abwicklung von Verzollung sowie Versandoptimierung. Dabei wird eng mit einem internen Tech-Team zusammengearbeitet, um Prozesse kontinuierlich weiterzuentwickeln. Die Arbeitsweise ist geprägt von Vertrauen, Engagement und dem direkten Austausch im Team. Bereit, Logistik neu zu denken? Dann freut sich unser Rocken Partner auf deine Bewerbung.

Verantwortung


Analyse von operativen Kunden- und Marktdaten mithilfe geeigneter statistischer Methoden
Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung von Machine-Learning- und Deep-Learning-Modellen zur Generierung neuer Erkenntnisse
Integration von Algorithmen in bestehende Applikationen und Systeme
Einsatz von Predictive Analytics zur Erstellung von Forecasts und datenbasierten Entscheidungsgrundlagen
Mitgestaltung und Weiterentwicklung des Bereichs Data Analytics innerhalb der Organisation


Qualifikationen


Abgeschlossenes Studium in einer technischen oder naturwissenschaftlichen Disziplin (z. B. Mathematik, Statistik, Informatik) oder gleichwertige Berufserfahrung
Erfahrung in Data Analytics, Data Science und/oder Data Engineering
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Python, R und SQL, idealerweise zusätzlich in C#
Von Vorteil Erfahrung in Datenvisualisierung mit Power BI oder Tableau
Freude an Kommunikation und Zusammenarbeit in Deutsch und Englisch


Benefits


Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung
Homeoffice
Zahlreiche Mitarbeiterevents
Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte
Markt- und leistungsgerechte Löhne
Attraktive Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten
Offene Unternehmenskultur
Eingespieltes und dynamisches Team


ROCKEN Jobs

https://rocken.jobs

Profil Erstellen

https://rocken.jobs/application/profil-erstellen/

Description:
Unternehmensbeschreibung

Wüest Partner ist ein innovatives und unabhängiges Software- und Dienstleistungsunternehmen der Immobilienwirtschaft. Seit 1985 schaffen wir fundierte Entscheidungsgrundlagen durch die Verbindung von Immobilien-Fachexpertise, Daten und digitalen Lösungen. Als Branchenführer gestalten wir gemeinsam mit unseren Kundinnen und Kunden die Zukunft der Immobilienbranche – mit umfassenden Leistungen in den Bereichen Bewertung, Beratung, Data, Analytics, KI, Software, Nachhaltigkeit und Bildung.

Die Wüest-Partner-Gruppe beschäftigt rund 500 Mitarbeitende an 15 Standorten in Europa mit Hauptsitz in der Schweiz. Zur Gruppe gehören die Schweizer Tochtergesellschaften Durable, Datahouse, Qualicasa und Signa-Terre.

Stellenbeschreibung

Dein Team und deine Mission

In der Division Data, Analytics & Technology bündeln wir Expertise in Datenanalyse, Modellierung, Softwareentwicklung und Immobilienmarktresearch.

Am Standort Zürich wirst du Teil des 9-köpfigen Data Teams Schweiz und arbeitest in offener und kooperativer Atmosphäre mit unseren erfahrenen Experten der Bereiche Data Science und Data Engineering zusammen. Unsere Mission ist die Entwicklung qualitativ hochwertiger Datensets, darauf aufbauenden Analysen und datengetriebenen Modellen, um die Produkte von Wüest Partner auf das nächste Level zu heben.

Deine Aufgaben


Aufbau und Entwicklung räumlicher und gebäudespezifischer Datenbestände und Modelle zum Immobilienmarkt, sowohl bezogen auf den Standort Schweiz, als auch im internationalen Kontext
Konzeption und kontinuierliche Optimierung von Data Pipelines in unserem internationalen Data Warehouse
Ausführen quantitativer und qualitativer Datenanalysen mit räumlichem Bezug
Länder- und teamübergreifende Zusammenarbeit zur Koordination und Lösungsfindung daten- und modellbezogener Fragestellungen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit unserer Division der Real Estate Experts zur Gewährleistung hoher Datenstandards im Bewertungskontext
Rolle als Expert/in und zentrale Ansprechperson für datenbezogene Fragestellungen
Monitoring und Dokumentation von Prozessen zur Sicherstellung der Datenqualität


Qualifikationen

Dein Profil


Hochschulabschluss mit analytischem oder technischem Schwerpunkt, Natur- oder Ingenieurwissenschaften
Sehr gute Programmierkenntnisse mit Python und R für die Schwerpunkte Statistik, Data Science und Data Processing
Nachweislich Erfahrung mit Databricks / Spark (PySpark, sparklyr, SparkR) - Zertifizierung wünschenswert
Solide Datenbank- und DWH Kenntnisse (inkl. SQL)
Erfahrung mit Bitbucket, Gitea und Versionierungstools, z.B. Git
Interesse an immobilienbezogenen Themen, Data Analytics, Machine Learning und AI
Eigeninitiative, selbständige und gut strukturierte Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägten analytischen und konzeptionellen Fähigkeiten
Teamorientierte Persönlichkeit mit hoher Sozial- und Kommunikationskompetenz
Verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse

Erfrischende, professionelle und kollegiale Unternehmenskultur mit tollen Events (Unternehmensfeiern, Gipfeli-Pausen etc.)

Title: Data Scientist (all genders welcome)
Description:
Location: Stans, NW, Switzerland

Apply now

What you can expect

For our development team at the Stans (CH) site we are looking for a

Data Scientist (all genders welcome)

Together with a small interdisciplinary team, you will ensure the rapid implementation of customer-specific product developments. These are inspection equipment for oil, gas and hydrogen pipelines as well as above ground storage tanks.

ACTIVITY:


Development of algorithms for the detection and classification of objects from data measured by our pipeline inspection equipment
Co-creation and implementation of technological trends, for example in the areas of artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, data persistence and processing, as well as big data solutions
Collecting, preparing, processing and analyzing large heterogeneous data sources
Development of algorithmic methods from the fields of signal processing, machine learning and computer vision and use of 3rd party components
Provision of algorithms and intelligent services in the form of minimum viable products with a focus on customer experience
Interdisciplinary cooperation in the integration of algorithms into our software applications


What you will bring

To become part of the ROSEN family, you will impress with an independent and reliable way of working. You will also bring with you:


Completed studies or doctorate in information technology, physics, mathematics, computer science or a comparable field of study
Sound knowledge of applied mathematics (linear algebra, optimization methods, stochastics)
Knowledge of at least one object-oriented programming language (e.g., Python, C/C++, or Java)
Methodological competencies in the field of data mining and machine learning, in particular supervised and unsupervised learning
Experience in algorithm development, e.g. for descriptive and diagnostic analyses, as well as with forecasting methods
Basic knowledge in the field of non-destructive testing using ultrasound as well as experience in the topics of explainable artificial intelligence, MLOps and applied statistics desirable
Experience in the field of software engineering is an advantage
Good written and spoken English language skills, German is an advantage


What we offer

We offer you an exciting and varied task in a highly interesting and international environment with appropriate resources. You can expect advanced working conditions in an innovative and future-oriented company.

Who we are The ROSEN Group is a leading global provider of cutting-edge solutions in all areas of the integrity process chain. Since its beginnings as a one-man business in 1981, ROSEN has grown rapidly and is today a technology group that operates in more than 110 countries with over 4,000 highly qualified employees.

ROSEN’s products and services:


Inspection of critical industrial assets to ensure reliable operations of the highest standards and effectiveness
Customized engineering consultancy providing efficient asset integrity management
Production and supply of customized novel products and systems
Market-driven, topical state-of-the-art research and development providing “added-value” products and services


For more information about the ROSEN Group, go to www.rosen-group.com.

Do you have any questions?

Kerstin Lange
